docs.text:

  • Search Engine: Use the local global search bar on the top-right corner to cross-reference NPCs, items, or spell acquisitions across all four indexs instantaneously.

docs.title: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen - Reference Guide
Key Characters & Factions
docs.text:
• Ispin Greenshield: A beloved, late adventurer known across Solamnia for his signature green shield and tall tales. His death serves as the initial catalyst for the party's gathering.
  • Becklin Uth Vaharin: A noble Knight of Solamnia and old companion of Ispin, stationed at Thornwall Keep in the village of Vogler.
  • Darrett Highwater: Becklin’s dedicated squire and later the party's primary military liaison officer within the Kalaman armed forces.
  • Cudgel Ironclad: The fierce leader of the Ironclad Regiment, a mercenary company initially hired for festival re-enactments who later becomes a core tactical ally.
  • The Dragon Army (Red Wing): A brutal, highly organized invading force marching under the banner of the Dragon Queen, utilizing reptilian Draconians, Hobgoblins, and terrifying siege machinery.
  • Dalamar the Dark: A mysterious and ambitious elven mage encountered in the Northern Wastes, seeking magical secrets while offering critical tactical intelligence to the party.

docs.title: Magical Inventions & Artifacts
docs.text:
• The Boilerdrak: A terrifying, dragon-shaped siege engine engineered by gnomes but corrupted by the Dragon Army to unleash devastating torrents of flame.
  • The Gnomeflinger: A chaotic, high-powered gnomish catapult designed for rapid personal transport or improvised defense.
  • The Fargab: A highly sophisticated, experimental gnomish communication device allowing long-distance vocal transmission.
  • The Dragonlance: A legendary, divine weapon forged specifically to combat dragons and their kin, recovered and repaired by the party over the course of the campaign.

docs.title: Regional Geography
docs.text:
• Vogler: A tranquil, riverside fishing village that serves as the tragic opening stage of the Dragon Army's invasion.
  • Kalaman: A heavily fortified port city, serving as the central bastion of defense and military command in the region.
  • The Northern Wastes: A barren, treacherous expanse of scarred earth, ancient ruins, and lethal wildlife hiding the path to the lost past.
  • The City of Lost Names (Onyari): A legendary, ruined flying city of pleasure built atop the remains of dead dragons, acting as the ultimate objective for both the party and the Dragon Army.

docs.title: Session 1: The Funeral and the First Blood
docs.text:
The adventure began with a somber invitation. The party received a letter from Becklin Uth Vaharin bearing the tragic news that your mutually cherished friend, the legendary adventurer Ispin Greenshield, had passed away. The letter cordially requested your presence at his upcoming funeral service in the peaceful village of Vogler. After converging and meeting up with the rest of your companions in the bustling city of Kalaman, you set out together along the highway toward Vogler. As you traveled, the air grew heavy with unsettling rumors from passing merchants whispering of an encroaching war in the east.

The gravity of these rumors manifested quickly. Along the road, a panicked young man came speeding toward you on horseback, shouting a frantic warning that the knights who had hired him as a guide had just been brutally ambushed by terrifying, reptilian-looking creatures just a short distance ahead. Springing into action, the party rushed forward to the scene of the attack. You engaged the strange monsters, successfully slaying two of the reptilian beasts while a trio of them managed to scatter and escape into the brush. With the immediate threat neutralized, you gathered yourselves and pressed onward to Vogler.

Upon arriving, you found a vibrant settlement preparing for a festival. You spent the afternoon exploring the town's local landmarks, pausing to visit the bustling docks of the Fishbowl and the welcoming atmosphere of the Brass Crab tavern. Your curiosity eventually led you to a sprawling mercenary camp pitched just north of the village. There you learned that these mercenaries, under the strict leadership of a commander named Cudgel, were scheduled to participate in a grand grand re-enactment of the historical Battle of the High Hill the following afternoon. During your tour, you were formally introduced to Becklin Uth Vaharin, Cudgel Ironclad, and several other local figures.

As twilight painted the river, the community gathered to send off Ispin Greenshield. Together with Becklin and Cudgel, you sent his funeral vessel down the water, transitioning into a lively reception filled with drinking and the sharing of Ispin's many legendary tall tales. The celebration was briefly disrupted by a pompous noble named Lord Bakaris, who loudly heckled the presenters, yawning dramatically and insulting the quality of the stories. Ignoring the noble's arrogance, Becklin pulled you aside to request that you pass by Thornwall Keep the next morning, revealing that Ispin had left a specific parting gift for the group. Over breakfast the following day, her squire, Barrett, presented you with Ispin’s beautiful, signature green shield.

By noon, the Mayor officially kicked off the village festival, and the party eagerly signed up for the local fishing competition. To the absolute astonishment of the crowd, Dave successfully reeled in the fabled "Benebog the Line Breaker," instantly cementing his status as a local celebrity throughout Vogler. Naturally, the miserable Lord Bakaris was present once again, doing his absolute best to sour the festive mood.

Shortly after the tournament concluded, the celebratory parade marched out toward High Hill to begin the grand re-enactment. The local town militia donned costumes to play the noble Knights of Solamnia, while Cudgel's mercenaries took up the mantle of the wicked forces of Istar. While both sides were strictly ordered to utilize blunt wooden props, the atmosphere shifted instantly as the lines clashed; the mercenaries were unmistakably wielding sharpened, lethal steel. A terrifyingly real battle erupted on the field. The party fought valiarly amidst the chaos, cutting through a contingent of treacherous mercenaries and subduing their ringleader, a half-orc named Gregaris. It quickly became clear that Gregaris had secretly bribed a massive portion of the mercenary company behind Cudgel's back. In the aftermath of the betrayal, the spectators were left utterly paralyzed with shock—most notably Cudgel, Mayor Raven, and Becklin. Deeply grateful for your swift intervention, the town council promised a handsome reward for saving the militia from a total massacre.

docs.title: Session 2: The Coming Storm
docs.text:
Following the harrowing betrayal at the Battle of the High Hill, the party's heroic efforts pushed them to new heights, and you officially attained level 3!

The group immediately set to work administering medical aid to the wounded villagers before securing one of the surviving traitorous mercenaries for a stern interrogation. Under your fierce questioning, the captive cracked and spilled his secrets. He revealed that Gregaris had ventured into a dense forest west of town a few days prior, returning with a massive iron coffer overflowing with gold coins. Using this sinister hoard, Gregaris had successfully bribed a large faction of Cudgel's men, ordering them to slaughter the town militia during the re-enactment as a precursor to a full-scale raid on the defenseless village.

Armed with this terrifying intelligence, the party marched directly toward the western forest to investigate. It did not take long to discover heavy tracks cutting north through the mud. Following the trail, you climbed a ridge and were horrified to behold a massive, heavily armed military host marching under an unknown banner. Before you could retreat, an advanced army scout patrol consisting of six fierce, reptilian creatures spotted you. A swift skirmish broke out, and the party successfully eliminated the patrol before sprinting back to Vogler to sound the alarm.

You immediately briefed the town council on the encroaching invasion. Cudgel returned shortly after, escorting a loyal mercenary captain named Jeyev, and both grimly confirmed that they had spotted the exact same invading army advancing through the wilderness. Moments later, a breathless messenger arrived at the River Gate bearing a formal ultimatum from the attackers: they were the vanguard of the Dragon Queen's Red Army, and they commanded the village to surrender and prepare to receive their occupying forces by nightfall.

Recognizing the desperate need for heavy military reinforcement, Becklin’s squire, Darrett, bravely volunteered to take a fast skiff down the river to Kalaman in a high-stakes bid to alert the grand marshal. Not long after his departure, the vanguard of the Dragon Army arrived, taking up threatening positions atop the ridges and just outside the main gates.

Utilizing a highly volatile gnomish contraption known as the Gnomeflinger, the party launched themselves across the lines directly into the mercenary camp. Through a brilliant display of tactical deception and quick-witted diplomacy, you managed to guide the remaining loyal mercenaries safely inside the village walls to reinforce the perimeter. Meanwhile, Dave took charge of the civilian population, meticulously organizing a massive evacuation effort to ferry the terrified villagers across to the safety of the river's north bank. In a quiet, heavy moment amidst the preparations, Becklin pulled you aside to confide that she had hidden a secure box beneath her bed back at Thornwall Keep; she made you swear that if she should fall in the coming battle, you would retrieve the box and deliver it directly to Darrett. With the enemy beating at the gates, Becklin, Cudgel, and the remaining mercenaries marched to the north gate to mount a desperate final defense, leaving the party to oversee the final phase of the civilian retreat. Seeking to maximize your defenses, you paid one last visit to the local inventor, Than, instructing him to calibrate and utilize the Gnomeflinger as a heavy piece of artillery should the defenses crumble.

docs.title: Session 3: The Fall of Vogler
docs.text:
As the vanguard of the Dragon Army began pounding against the village defenses, Boycott slipped away to Thornwall Keep to fulfill the urgent task Becklin had requested. Sneaking into her private quarters, Boycott located the hidden box beneath the frame. Peeking inside, he discovered a magnificent, pristine suit of Solamnic plate armor, but opted to leave it secure in its hiding place for the time being. Looking out from the high windows of the keep, he watched in horror as the main body of the invading army came marching down the highway like a crimson wave. Boycott sprinted back through the cobblestone streets to rejoin the main defense force.

Just as he arrived, a battered mercenary came sprinting through the lines to deliver a dire report: the invading host had split into a deadly two-pronged assault, with one faction driving hard for the docks to cut off the escape boats while the other smashed into the mercenary lines. Before the warning could even register, a wing of terrifying, winged monsters known as Draconians swooped out of the smoky sky to attack. The party coordinated beautifully, utilizing swift steel and arcane power to quickly dispatch the reptilian beasts.

The retreat quickly devolved into a desperate, shifting urban warzone. You cut your way through successive waves of invading Draconians, pausing in shock as you spotted three mysterious, heavily armored knights watching the carnage from the clifftops before vanishing into thin air. Amidst the chaos of the burning buildings, a stray, terrified cat leaped directly onto Snub's chest, clinging to his armor with white-knuckled grip; Snub instantly fell in love, naming the brave feline Marmaduke, and the two became utterly inseparable for the remainder of the campaign.

The party pushed through the smoke toward the wharf, where the village was being completely overrun. There, a final defensive line of Draconians had deployed a terrifying piece of gnomish siege machinery known as a Boilerdrak, which spewed torrents of liquid flame across the docks. You launched a desperate assault, cutting down the operators and disabling the infernal machine. Just as the final civilian transports were casting off, the brutal commander of the vanguard, Fewmaster Gholcag, stormed the wharf. A brutal, bloody boss battle erupted, leaving multiple party members unconscious and bleeding out on the wooden planks. Through sheer determination and clutch healing, you rallied and brought the warlord crashing down.

With the village thoroughly overrun and completely engulfed in flames, you leaped onto the very last boat casting off from the shore. As the skiff drifted into the current, the mercenary captain Jeyev came splashing through the shallows, clambering aboard with a shattered officer's helmet clutched tightly in his hands. Together, you floated down the river, watching in heartbroken silence as Vogler burned to ash behind you. Through this baptism of fire, your shared hardships forged you into true heroes, and you officially achieved level 4!

docs.title: Session 4: The Special Taskforce of Kalaman
docs.text:
Following a long, cold night drifting down the river, your ragged fleet finally reached the shores near the grand fortified city of Kalaman. The coastline was a scene of utter despair; hundreds of Vogler refugees were dragging their crude fishing boats onto the mud, desperately attempting to spark campfires, gather food, and erect makeshift shelters against the elements. Amidst the chaos on the shoreline, you successfully reunited with Darrett, who was completely devastated to learn that his noble mentor, Becklin, had perished defending the gates. He informed you that while he had established initial contact with the Kalaman leadership, the high council was extremely eager to interview the party directly to gather firsthand intelligence on the invading force.

Once you ensured that the refugees were as stable and comfortable as possible under the circumstances, you marched into the city alongside Darrett, heading directly for the imposing spires of Kalaman Castle. Upon entering the grand audience chamber, you were highly annoyed to find that the manipulative Lord Bakaris and his arrogant son had already beaten you to the city, actively whispering in the ears of the leadership to push their own selfish political agendas.

The party stepped forward to brief the council, addressing the Governor, Calof Miat, the local Guild Leaders, and the stern Grand Marshal, Nestra Vendri. Impressed by your military competence and desperate for actionable intelligence, the council agreed to officially take in the Vogler refugees and provide them with proper municipal lodging—on the strict condition that the party enlist as a specialized military taskforce dedicated to high-stakes special operations against the Dragon Army threat. You accepted the terms, were assigned private quarters within the castle barracks, received a handsome advance in pay, and were granted official military insignias identifying your elite status. To your delight, Darrett was officially designated as your primary military liaison officer to manage your upcoming assignments.

With coin in hand, the party spent the afternoon conducting an extensive shopping and weapon-upgrading excursion at the famous Twin Hammerstrike Forges. During the break, Boycott successfully channeled a secret magical recipe passed down from his beloved Nannan, casting a sophisticated ritual spell to permanently summon a beautiful celestial spirit in the form of a helpful owl.

The following morning, Darrett delivered your very first official deployment. The military leadership was deeply alarmed by your reports of the fire-spewing Boilerdrak and desperately wished to analyze its design. You were ordered to seek out an eccentric gnomish engineer named Tatina Rookledust, who resided in the nearby hills and was rumored to be a foremost expert on such experimental machinery.

The party loaded into a horse-drawn supply cart and trotted out into the countryside. However, as you approached Rookledust’s workshop, you found her hilltop estate completely overrun by a raiding party of vicious Goblins. The party deployed rapidly from the cart, swiftly dispatching the raiders with practiced efficiency before knocking on the laboratory doors. Upon meeting Tatina, the quirky inventor was profoundly saddened to learn that her peaceful invention—which she had originally designed to function as an automated lawnmower—had been weaponized by the Dragon Army to burn down a village. Overjoyed by your rescue and eager to assist the war effort, she happily packed up her tools to accompany you back to Kalaman, presenting the party with her latest prototype communication device: a pair of miraculous, long-distance vocal transceivers known as the "Fargab." Together, you loaded her gear into the wagon and began the journey back to the city walls.

docs.title: Session 5: The Ashes of the Frontier
docs.text:
Upon your victorious return to the gates of Kalaman alongside Tatina Rookledust, your first order of business was escorting your sole goblin prisoner, affectionately dubbed Jim the Goblin, down into the high-security cells beneath Kalaman Castle. With your duty fulfilled for the day, the party headed into the city's entertainment district to spend a long, celebratory night drinking heavily at the popular Wanderer’s Rest tavern. Amidst the rowdy atmosphere, you struck up a conversation with a local blacksmith named Gareth, who shared a troubling rumor regarding a local fisherman named John who operated a vessel called the Mayflower. According to Gareth, John had recently been offered a massive sum of gold by mysterious agents to enlist as a high-ranking recruit within the Dragon Army.

The remainder of the evening devolved into pure, unadulterated revelry alongside the surprisingly wild Tatina Rookledust. The ale flowed freely, and at a certain point in the night, members of the party were even seen dancing wildly atop the tavern tables to the cheers of the patrons.

The inevitable consequence arrived the following morning in the form of a massive, pounding hangover for the entire taskforce. The pain was quickly cut short as Darrett arrived with an urgent deployment: a panicked farming family had just fled into the city gates, reporting that their frontier estate had been seized by an advanced scouting element of the Dragon Army. Following Darrett's precise tactical orders, you shook off the alcohol and marched out to the frontier farmstead.

Arriving at the property, you encountered a ruthless Dragon Army officer backed by a disciplined squad of six fierce Hobgoblins who were busy fortifying the farmhouse. A fierce, tactical skirmish ensued. During the fighting, you successfully captured a heavily scarred hobgoblin whom you securely bound and labeled "Jobbo the Burned." The party eliminated the officer and the remaining troops, but during the frantic melee, the structure was accidentally set ablaze, burning the farmhouse to the ground. Fortunately, the family's brave hound, Nono the dog, managed to escape the flames completely unharmed, bringing a bright spot of joy to the grim afternoon. Through this successful defense of the frontier, the party's growing combat synergy pushed them to a major milestone, and you officially attained level 5!

On the road back to Kalaman, you were surprised to cross paths with a familiar face: Cudgel Ironclad. The battle-weary mercenary commander explained that she and her tiny accompanying escort were quite literally all that remained of her legendary Ironclad Regiment. Following their catastrophic defeat at the fall of Vogler, they had been forced to navigate a grueling, winding route through the wilderness to evade enemy scouts and make it back to civilization.

Upon returning to headquarters, you immediately met with Marshal Vendri to deliver your report. Recognizing the growing proximity of the threat, the Marshal agreed to significantly increase the city's frontier defenses, provided the party immediately march out to investigate and scout a critical choke point known as Wheelwatch Outpost. Darrett was instructed to prepare all the necessary maps and tactical briefs for a dawn deployment.

docs.title: Session 6: The Infiltration of Wheelwatch Outpost
docs.text:
The next morning, Darrett gathered the party around the war table to deliver a grim update: fresh intelligence received at dawn confirmed that Wheelwatch Outpost had just been completely overrun and occupied by the vanguard of the Dragon Army. The high command officially ordered the Benebog Slayers to march out and liberate the strategic stone fortress, operating in perfect coordination with the surviving remnants of Cudgel’s loyal mercenaries.

During the journey out to the frontier, Cudgel rode alongside the party, providing a meticulous, room-by-room breakdown of the fortress's architectural layout and defensive weak points. Upon nearing the objective, you carefully steered your supply carts off the road, concealing them deep within the thick brush roughly one mile from the fortress walls to avoid detection.

Studying the battlements, you opted to implement a daring, two-pronged tactical assault. The party split into two specialized teams: an elite infiltration squad consisting of Blip, Boycott, and Snub would attempt to scale the walls under the cover of darkness to seize the gatehouse, while a heavy assault force led by Dave and Cudgel would wait in the tree line, prepared to storm the courtyard the exact moment the main gates were swung open.

The infiltration team executed their climb flawlessly, silently dropping onto the stone ramparts. They immediately crossed paths with a handful of sentries, eliminating them quietly before they could raise an alarm. However, the plan hit a major snag when they struck down a high-ranking Draconian sentry; upon its death, the creature’s dark magic triggered a screaming, auditory illusion that echoed across the battlements, threatening to alert the entire garrison. Thinking incredibly fast, Snub utilized brilliant roleplay and a cunning vocal deception to completely fool the approaching guards, convincing them it was a false alarm. With the garrison pacified, the trio methodically swept through the western side of the fortress, quietly dispatching every single enemy in the sector.

With the area temporarily secured, a disguised Snub embarked on a daring solo tour of the fortress interior. He successfully mapped the central structure, spotting an oblivious officer busy drafting a military report and locating a pair of mistreated prisoners locked in the cells. Slipping into the officer's barracks, Snub successfully looted a cache containing sixty gold pieces along with the master heavy iron key to the main gate mechanism.

Moving swiftly to the gatehouse, the infiltration team turned the heavy iron winches, throwing the massive wooden gates wide open. Dave, Cudgel, and the wave of loyal mercenaries came flooding out of the tree line like an avalanche, spilling into the courtyard and completely overwhelming the occupied fortress. The garrison was entirely put to the sword, save for a single, terrified soldier whom the party chose to disarm and capture for later interrogation.

docs.title: Session 7: The Blood of Steel Springs
docs.text:
Following the total liberation of the fortress, Commander Cudgel officially established Wheelwatch Outpost as her new base of operations. She provided the party with an official military dispatch to deliver back to Marshal Vendri in Kalaman. Before departing, you descended to the dungeons to free the captives, identifying them as Lanal Brint and Elgo Duckditcher, kindly returning a stolen hoopak weapon to her possession. You then conducted a brief interrogation of your captured soldier; after extracting a few fragmented pieces of intelligence regarding the Dragon Army's movements, the party swiftly executed him to ensure no leaks.

Your return journey to the city of Kalaman was entirely uneventful. However, upon entering the command castle, you were alarmed to discover that Marshal Vendri had unexpectedly marched out to the far west, taking over half of the city's active military force with her. The remaining garrison had been hastily deployed to strike down a rogue enemy contingent that had broken away from the main host. Shockingly, this secondary force was being led by the incompetent Lord Bakaris and his arrogant son, with your liaison officer Darrett riding alongside them.

The battle plan was to ambush the Red Dragon Army near a strategic river crossing of the Inkwater river located thirty miles west of Kalaman, a site known locally as Steel Springs. Finding a frantic, handwritten tactical note left behind by Darrett, the party immediately mobilized, racing across the countryside toward the coordinates.

Upon arriving at Steel Springs, you were thrown into a chaotic, raging battlefield. The party plunged into the fray, engaging a disciplined troop of mounted cavalry soldiers backed by a terrifying, flying reptilian beast known as a Dragonnel. A brutal, muddy skirmish ensued, forcing the enemy commander into a desperate tactical retreat. Following the retreating lines for six miles east of Steel Springs, you finally located Darrett’s hidden command encampment.

Safe within the trenches, Darrett explained the full tactical reality of the situation, confirming that Bakaris's arrogance had severely jeopardized the operation. After a long night's rest in the camp, Darrett encouraged the characters to immediately ride back to Kalaman to brief Governor Miat on the shifting front lines.

The party sped back to the city, hailing the guards to have the heavy portcullis raised. Wishing to remain useful, Dave temporarily left the main group to assist the municipal engineers in constructing a massive defensive wall to protect the incoming waves of frontier evacuees. The remaining party members marched into the castle, only to find their path barred by a pair of towering, ancient knights guarding the heavy doors to the council chamber. Refusing to tolerate the delay, Blip aggressively attempted to shove past the guards, instantly triggering a curse as the armor rattled to life—and we rolled initiative!

docs.title: Session 8: The Shadow of Caradoc
docs.text:
The confrontation outside the council chambers exploded into a frantic melee as the two suits of armor revealed themselves to be powerful, ancient skeleton knights. The party traded heavy blows with the undead guardians, ultimately smashing the bones to splinters. The very moment the final knight fell, Dave came bursting into the corridor, remarkably carrying an incredibly confused and disoriented warlock named Zarama over his broad shoulders. Zarama was utterly bewildered, having absolutely no idea why Dave had suddenly scooped her up from the city streets and dragged her into a active military castle.

Brushing off the confusion, the unified party threw open the heavy oak doors and marched into the grand audience chamber, finding the room completely frozen in a supernatural twilight. Waiting for you in the shadows was the sinister spirit of Caradoc.

A fast and lethal battle erupted. The party deployed their full martial capabilities, rapidly cutting through Caradoc’s physical knight form. However, the moment his physical shell collapsed, his true, malevolent spirit form rose from the corpse. Unleashing a wave of dark psychic energy, Caradoc seized absolute telepathic control over Dave’s mind, forcing the mighty warrior to turn his weapons upon his own companions. A tragic, chaotic frenzy followed, during which Dave tragically perished twice—once voluntarily to break the mental possession, and once forcefully amidst the crossfire.

Through grit and divine intervention, the party successfully exorcised and destroyed Caradoc's spirit form. As his essence dissolved into the ether, his echoing voice promised that he would meet the characters again in the afterlife.

With quiet restored to the bloody audience chamber, a sharp-eyed party member noticed a fresh, crimson trail of blood smudged across the stone floor in the far corner of the room. Following the gory tracks down a forgotten maintenance corridor, you discovered an area where a heavy, bricked-up wall had recently been violently knocked down from the inside. Peering through the dust, bricks, and rubble, you beheld a ancient flight of stone stairs descending deep into the subterranean darkness below the castle, where a faint, eerie violet illumination was visible pulsing far down in the depths.

docs.title: Session 9: The Catacombs of Sarlamir
docs.text:
Following the faint, pulsing violet illumination, the party cautiously descended the ancient stone staircase into the deep catacombs stretching far beneath Kalaman Castle. As you ventured through the damp, subterranean crypts, the swirling violet mist flared to life, forcing the party to witness a series of vivid, historical illusions depicting the tragic life story of the legendary death knight, Lord Soth. The exploration was violently interrupted as a pack of malevolent wraiths materialized from the stone sarcophagi, but the party coordinated effectively and swiftly banished the spirits.

Marching deeper into the complex, you discovered a series of massive, jagged holes that had been brutally blasted directly through the ancient reinforced walls of the catacombs, all heading in a definitive southward trajectory. Amidst the debris of a forgotten vault, the party uncovered a trove of rare historical equipment, including a +1 longsword, a pristine steel breastplate, and a beautifully woven Kagonesti Forest Shroud made entirely of living leaves. Dave spent some time interacting with a pair of ancient warhorse skeletons resting in an alcove, successfully earning a divine blessing known as the "Favor of the Heroic Steed."

The party eventually discovered a hidden subterranean shrine, where you crossed paths with a peaceful spectral spirit who introduced herself as Jandin. She shared deep historical lore regarding the legendary Knight Sarlamir, explaining how his pride, ultimate disobedience, and wicked deeds during the age of the Cataclysm had brought a permanent curse upon his bloodline. She noted that the eerie, supernatural flames dancing across the tombs were the literal leftover spiritual embers of that ancient divine wrath.

The group pressed forward into the grand central tomb of Sarlamir himself. The moment you crossed the threshold, you were pulled into a powerful historical vision: you watched a spectral Lord Soth approach a roaring brazier, touching the flames with a dark scepter carved with screaming faces. Suddenly, the vision shattered as the skeletal form of Lord Sarlamir rose furiously from his stone sarcophagus to attack. A fierce boss battle ensued, during which the undead knight bellowed about Lord Soth, claiming the death knight had beckoned him to march back north to the legendary "City of Lost Names"—the precise site of his historical dishonor.

With a final, coordinated strike, the party defeated Sarlamir, causing his bones to turn to ash. The very moment he was laid to rest, the supernatural violet flames dancing across his weapon and throughout the catacombs instantly vanished. Searching the empty sarcophagus, you recovered a singular, heavy relic: the rusty iron head of an ancient spear.

This monumental victory pushed the party's capabilities to an elite status, and you all gained a level, officially reaching level 6!

Realizing that Lord Soth had already escaped the catacombs long before you arrived, you ascended back to the surface. You found that Darrett had just arrived back within the city walls alongside Marshal Vendri, Lord Bakaris, and the bulk of the Kalaman army. Marshal Vendri immediately moved to establish strict martial law to keep order in the panicked city, while a fascinated Darrett pulled the party aside, deeply eager to learn every single detail regarding Lord Soth and the mysterious City of Lost Names.

docs.title: Session 10: Into the Northern Wastes
docs.text:
Following your harrowing expedition through the subterranean catacombs, Darrett formally approached the party to invite you to an emergency war council with Marshal Vendri. Before attending the high-stakes meeting, however, the exhausted taskforce secured a comfortable, uninterrupted night's rest to fully recover their strength. During the quiet evening hours, Boycott sat in deep meditation, successfully attuning to the magical Kagonesti Forest Shroud you had recovered from the crypts.

The next morning, you marched into the war room to meet with Marshal Vendri. While the battle-hardened Marshal was initially highly unenthusiastic about chasing legends, Darrett delivered a brilliant tactical argument that successfully convinced her that the remote Northern Wastes required an immediate, heavy military investigation. To prepare for the expedition, the party spent the afternoon researching within the castle's grand library, successfully uncovering a rare historical text titled The Rough Guide to the Northern Wastes along with a detailed geographical map of the uncharted region.

During your research, the arrogant Lord Bakaris began causing trouble once again, attempting to disrupt your preparations. To neutralize the nuisance, the party paid a band of local street urchins twenty-five gold pieces to aggressively terrorize and keep a watchful eye on the noble for the next seven days. To maximize the psychological warfare, you even provided the kids with a massive glass jar filled entirely with Marmaduke’s pungent cat urine to utilize during their pranks. Before departing, you dropped off your legendary Dragonlance at the Twin Hammerstrike Forges for extensive repairs, with the blacksmiths promising it would be fully restored in exactly one week.

With assignments in hand, Marshal Vendri officially deployed the characters to the Northern Wastes to uncover the Dragon Army’s ultimate agenda. The party set sail from Kalaman harbor aboard a heavy military transport, accompanied by both Cudgel and Tatina Rookledust, who had proudly volunteered to join the dangerous voyage.

The fleet eventually dropped anchor near a treacherous coastal region known as Wrecker's Edge. The party loaded into a small rowboat to scout the coastline for a secure beachhead to land the larger troop ships. During the scouting run, you spotted an abandoned sailing vessel drifting in the mist and boarded it to investigate. Unfortunately, you accidentally triggered a magical pressure trap on the deck, causing two roaring Air Elementals to materialize and attack. The party fought through the wind vortexes, dispersing the elementals.

Suddenly, a mysterious elven mage named Dalamar materialized on the deck. He introduced himself and openly shared his reasons for exploring the wastes, politely extending an exclusive invitation for the party to visit his hidden elven encampment. With the coast clear, the military ships successfully landed, and the Kalaman army established a heavily fortified base camp at Wrecker's Edge, intending to march toward the elven camp at first light.

The next morning, as you journeyed through the scarred terrain toward the coordinates, your path was blocked by a patrol of Dragon Army soldiers backed by a fierce Draconian officer. The party responded instantly, deploying a barrage of devastating fireball spells that rapidly incinerated the enemies.

Upon entering the elven camp, you were greeted coldly by their leader, Zhelsuel, who was completely unhappy to see human military forces invading their sacred lands. Frustrated by his leader's stubborn isolationism, Dalamar pulled the party aside and made a secret, mutually beneficial deal: he promised to share all of his hard-earned historical research in exchange for the party's tactical assistance in the field. Dalamar stepped up to your war map and marked three precise archaeological locations that he believed held the key to the region's magic: the Blue Phoenix Shrine, the Sunward Fortress, and the obelisk of Wakenreth. Eager to begin, the party set off toward the Blue Phoenix Shrine, with Dalamar riding alongside you as a guide.

docs.title: Session 11: The Obelisk of Wakenreth
docs.text:
The journey toward the Blue Phoenix Shrine was filled with academic debate. As you traveled across the desolate landscape, Dalamar chatted extensively with the group, analyzing old ruins and trying to gauge whether any of you possessed a genuine passion for ancient history. Blip seemed to take a strong, immediate liking to the charismatic elven mage, and the two spent the march getting along remarkably well.

Upon nearing the sacred perimeter of the shrine, the party crossed paths with a small band of displaced Sea Elves. Their vanguard leader introduced himself as Ishvern Stargazer, explaining that his people were attempting to complete their traditional annual pilgrimage to the temple. Tragically, they had been ambushed by a advance force of the Dragon Army, and their high priestess, Yearkal, had been captured and dragged away into the wastes. Ishvern passionately pleaded for the party's assistance in completing their sacred offering, promising a handsome reward for your protection.

Ishvern accompanied the group inside the Blue Phoenix Shrine, guiding you through the weathered architecture, though he admitted he knew very little of the specific rituals as Yearkal had always handled the spiritual mechanics. The party pushed into the absolute inner sanctum of the temple, reverently placing their offering of shimmering blue pearls upon the ancient altar. In recognition of your piety, the altar flared with divine light, and Ishvern rewarded each party member with a matching blue pearl. Suddenly, a warm sensation washed over the group as you received Habbakuk’s divine blessing, permanently bolstering your survival capabilities as you traveled through the harsh Northern Wastes. As you exited the shrine, a sea elf scout came racing back from the desert, reporting that they had successfully tracked the captured priestess to a fortified enemy outpost known as Camp Carrionclay, located near the murky waters of Sky's Tears.

Wishing to secure the remaining marked locations before launching a rescue mission, the party altered their trajectory and marched directly toward the ancient obelisk of Wakenreth. Arriving at the site, you were stunned by a magnificent display of broken magic: massive stone blocks were floating perfectly locked in place in mid-air, levitating around the perimeter of a broken tower.

The party stepped through the threshold of the mystical tower, discovering a grand central chamber dominated by a massive stone statue of an elven warrior surrounded by hundreds of glinting silver coins. Seizing the financial opportunity, Dave spent twenty minutes methodically picking up every single piece of silver on the floor.

Ascending a spiral staircase to the second level of the tower, the party was suddenly blindsided by two vengeful wraiths. A fast, frantic battle followed; once you defeated the spirits, the ghost of a grey-haired woman detached herself from the ectoplasm and floated gracefully up through the ceiling to the chamber above, beckoning the party to follow her.

docs.title: Session 12: The Dancing Longsword and the Worm
docs.text:
Following the ghostly apparition up to the third level of the Wakenreth tower, the party found themselves face-to-face with a peaceful, floating spirit. The phantom identified herself as Jentida, explaining that she had originally served as a guard for a historical elven expedition sent to the wastes centuries ago. She anxiously inquired if the party had ever crossed paths with her sister, Tenadria, a renowned elven diplomat in the civilized lands. Recognizing the party's noble intent, Jentida floated over to Dave and formally presented him with her ancient family heirloom: a magnificent, magical Dancing Longsword.

The party continued their ascent to the absolute apex of the Wakenreth tower, discovering a swirling, highly volatile magical portal humming at the center of the platform. Driven by reckless curiosity, Dave stepped forward and began tampering with the portal controls multiple times. His interference destabilized the energy field, causing a massive, extraplanar monstrosity to tear through the rift and attack. A frantic, high-stakes battle ensued on the high platform, but the party fought with perfect cohesion, ultimately slaying the beast and successfully sealing the rift. With the portal safely closed and the tower's levitating architecture permanently restored to structural stability, Dalamar expressed a desire to remain behind to conduct a meticulous, long-term investigation of the local runes.

The remaining party members packed their gear and set out across the desert toward the final marked location: the Sunward Fortress. However, as you navigated a series of deep sand dunes, the earth suddenly shattered beneath your feet as a colossal, ravenous Purple Worm burst from the ground to ambush the group. The combat was terrifyingly brutal; multiple party members were swallowed whole and ingested into the monster's acidic stomach, but through desperate internal strikes and heavy external magic, you managed to slay the titanic worm and drag your battered companions out of its carcass.

Wiping the worm slime from your armor, you pressed forward through the wastes, finally arriving at the gates of the Sunward Fortress, where the vanguard of the Kalaman army was waiting anxiously for your arrival.

docs.title: Session 13: Slaadi In the Deep
docs.text:
The party remained deployed deep within the dangerous expanses of the Northern Wastes, desperately trying to uncover the Red Dragon Army’s ultimate intentions. As you recall, a massive faction of the enemy host had inexplicably marched into this barren wasteland, and your taskforce was specifically assigned to determine what they were looking for. With your archaeological companion Dalamar still hard at work deciphering runes back at Wakenreth, the party gathered around the command table at the Sunward Fortress to brief Darrett and Cudgel on your findings. After a brief tactical discussion, it was decided that the party would head down into the fortress's massive subterranean excavation site to investigate.

Approaching the dark entrance of the cavern complex, you discovered a pristine, glowing pool of mystical fluid. After offering a collective prayer to the gods of good, the liquid resonated, and you successfully scooped up several vials of potent fire breath liquid.

Descending into the damp, excavated tunnels, the party was immediately ambushed by a squad of grotesque, mutated Draconians. A fierce, bloody skirmish followed, during which you successfully eliminated the mutations. However, during a post-combat medical sweep, you made a horrifying discovery: Boycott had been thoroughly infected with an alien parasite by a Slaad, and the rest of the party had to work quickly using daggers to painfully excise a cluster of squirming alien eggs nestled just beneath the surface of their own skin.

Pushing deeper into the subterranean tunnels, you discovered a hidden holding cell and successfully rescued the captured sea elf priestess, Yearkal, while administering vital healing magic to a severely battered local guide named Rone. After helping them to their feet, you escorted the survivors back up to the surface to the safety of the army lines. Deeply grateful for his salvation, Rone offered the party a staggering reward of five hundred gold pieces on the strict condition that you escort him safely back to his hidden home village of Heart's Hollow.

The party returned to the depths to complete the purge of the cavern complex. Slipping into the lowest chamber, you engaged and defeated a vicious pack of fully grown Slaadi. Hidden behind their nesting grounds, you uncovered the ultimate prize: a massive, glowing orange crystal pulsing with raw, volatile magical energy. Through these harrowing subterranean trials, the party's combat mastery reached an elite threshold, and you officially achieved level 7!

docs.title: Session 14: Arrival at Heart's Hollow
docs.text:
The party continued their dangerous expedition across the Northern Wastes, driven by the firm belief that locating the mythical City of Lost Names held the ultimate clue to the Dragon Army's presence in the region. Back at the obelisk, Dalamar had sent word via a sending spell that he believed he could pinpoint the exact coordinates of the ruined flying city by systematically translating the writings on the tower walls.

Before departing the Sunward Fortress, the party conducted a final, meticulous sweep of the deep cavern where you had slain the warlord Viruzza. Your search yielded a fascinating trove of rare items, including a handful of pulsing orange stones, a powerful wand of the war mage, and a bundle of handwritten military dispatches. Reading through the captured notes, the horrific agenda of the Red Dragon Army was finally laid bare: they were utilizing the massive, glowing orange crystal in the cave to forcefully mutate innocent captives, transforming them into loyal Draconian soldiers. Suddenly, Snub stepped forward and affectionately hugged the massive orange stone; he immediately fell violently ill, and over the next forty-eight hours, his physical condition deteriorated rapidly as a supernatural sickness took hold of his body.

Ascending to the surface, you held a final briefing with Yearkal and Rone. Yearkal was deeply intrigued to hear of your alliance with Ishvern's pilgrimage, but she tearfully requested that you keep an eye out for several more missing elves who were still unaccounted for. Rone reiterated his desperate plea, offering five hundred gold pieces to act as a security escort to his secret, subterranean sanctuary of Heart's Hollow.

The party loaded Rone into a supply cart and set off across the wastes, with Snub growing increasingly feverish and pale in the back of the wagon. On the second day of travel, you successfully spotted and ambushed a Red Dragon Army desert patrol, wiping them out completely before they could raise an alarm.

After two grueling days of travel through the canyons, you finally neared the coordinates of the hidden settlement. Just as you approached a massive geological formation known as The Crevasse, you heard frantic shouting; a local scavenger named Clystran was sprinting for his life, pursued by five monstrous Giant Spiders. The party deployed instantly from the cart, slicing through the arachnids with practiced efficiency. Overjoyed by your timely intervention, a breathless Clystran explained that he had lived in the hidden village of Heart's Hollow his entire life, and he would be absolutely delighted to guide you and Rone safely through the secret mountain passes. Following his lead down a series of concealed tunnels, the party finally stepped out into the breathtaking, subterranean oasis of Heart's Hollow!

docs.title: Session 15: The Secret of Bluemaw Cave
docs.text:
Upon entering the beautiful, hidden sanctuary of Heart's Hollow, your first priority was escorting the wounded Rone safely back to his family home. Upon arrival, his mother embraced her son tearfully and presented the party with a reward of four hundred gold pieces—noting with an apologetic grimace that it was exactly one hundred gold less than originally agreed due to their current financial hardships. To make amends, she treated the entire famished taskforce to a massive, steaming shepherd's pie, proudly declaring it to be the ultimate culinary specialty of Heart's Hollow.

With your bellies full, you immediately carried the shivering, delirious Snub down to the bottom of the town's grand Mosaic structure to see Lavender, a highly eccentric local mage and potion brewer. After analyzing Snub's condition, Lavender suggested a casting of lesser restoration, which successfully purged the orange crystal toxins and restored the rogue to perfect health. Seizing the opportunity, the party spent a portion of their reward money to purchase a handful of rare alchemical mixtures from her shelves, including several valuable potions of clairvoyance.

Later that afternoon, the party approached the central town notice board to scout for local contract work. You located a prominent flyer detailing a family of desperate Dwarves who were in dire need of immediate military assistance, with instructions to interview the local innkeeper to gather the specifics. The innkeeper revealed that a clan of dwarves had been forcefully driven out of their ancestral home at Bluemaw Cave by invading forces.

The party gathered their gear and marched out toward the coordinates of Bluemaw Cave. As you neared the dark cavern entrance, Snub uncorked and drank one of his newly acquired potions of clairvoyance, granting him a vivid, magical mental layout of the entire cave structure. Pushing into the shadows, you narrowly avoided stepping directly into a deadly mechanical trap. Suddenly, a dwarf woman named Grasha stepped out from the tunnels, sincerely apologizing for almost blowing you up. She explained that she had laid the traps to combat a raiding party of Dragon Army Draconians who had been methodically looting the cave, searching for the legendary lost hoard of a long-gone blue dragon. The party made a swift contractual agreement: you promised to completely eradicate the invaders in exchange for the remainder of the dragon's treasure hoard.

The group advanced deep into the cavern chambers. You first encountered and sliced through a ravenous pack of subterranean Gricks before cornering the looting Draconians in the main vault. The party unleashed a devastating barrage of high-level fireball spells, completely incinerating the enemy forces and securing the cavern.

docs.title: Session 16: The Raid on Camp Carrionclay
docs.text:
Following the total eradication of the Draconian forces within Bluemaw Cave, the party formally concluded their contract with Grasha, bidding the dwarves farewell as you loaded your gear back into the supply carts. Your next objective was clear: you must march on the fortified enemy outpost of Camp Carrionclay to liberate the captured Sea Elves.

However, as your wagon train crossed a massive alkali flat, the desert floor exploded once again as another titanic Purple Worm surged from the depths to attack. A brutal, high-stakes battle erupted in the sand dunes. In the absolute heat of the melee, a bizarre magical anomaly occurred, causing Snub to suddenly and unexpectedly transform into a powerful beast form. Through sheer martial violence, the party ultimately brought the massive worm crashing down. Searching the nearby terrain after the battle, you uncovered a pair of highly mysterious alchemical potions buried in the sand.

Resuming your march, the party finally arrived at the rocky ridges overlooking the heavily fortified perimeter of Camp Carrionclay. Seeking a tactical advantage, Snub drank another potion of clairvoyance, gaining a perfect, detailed layout of the entire enemy garrison, sentry rotations, and prison cages.

To breach the heavily guarded compound, the party devised a chaotic tactical diversion. Slipping through the shadows under the cover of dusk, you systematically set fire to a dozen storage tents along the western wall, sending a roaring inferno into the sky and throwing the garrison into absolute panic. Amidst the spreading smoke and confusion, Dave executed a incredibly cunning, high-stakes ruse: he walked directly up to the front gate and hand-delivered Blip over to the guards as a "captured prisoner." Once Blip was successfully escorted past the throat of the defenses, the trap was sprung. The party launched a total, uncompromising assault from the smoke, cutting off all avenues of escape and systematically wiping out the entire enemy camp in a masterclass of military violence.

docs.title: Session 17: The Road to Onyari
docs.text:
Date: September 20, 2025

The aftermath of the total destruction of Camp Carrionclay yielded an incredible series of triumphs. Pushing into the command tents, the party discovered a massive, unhatched dragon egg resting in an arcane incubator. Moments later, you smashed the iron locks on the slave cages, successfully liberating the captured Sea Elves alongside a joyous band of captive Kender. You also managed to free a captured, massive Bulette beast from its training pins. Suddenly, a terrifying adult Black Dragon descended from the storm clouds, landing in the center of the camp. Showing incredible bravery and quick wit, Snub stepped forward and presented the unhatched dragon egg to the massive beast as a peaceful offering; deeply respected by the gesture, the dragon claimed its offspring and presented Snub with a massive, magically resonant dragon scale before soaring back into the sky.

With the frontier cleared, the party rushed back to the obelisk of Wakenreth to reconvene with Dalamar. The elven mage welcomed you with a triumphant smile, revealing that his tireless translations of the ancient runes had successfully pinpointed the exact, hidden coordinates of the legendary flying city: the City of Lost Names (Onyari).

The taskforce immediately mobilized the Kalaman army, marching across the final stretch of the wastes toward the coordinates. Along the way, the earth trembled as a third massive Purple Worm lunged from the dunes, but the party was now entirely practiced in giant-monster combat, rapidly slicing the beast to pieces without breaking stride.

At long last, you arrived at the colossal canyon hiding the ruins of the City of Lost Names—only to find your path completely blocked by the massive, heavily fortified main siege body of the Red Dragon Army. Suddenly, Darrett Highwater came riding through the dunes to meet you. To your immense delight, he was accompanied by a military transport bearing custom DHL packages from Kalaman: inside were your fully repaired, legendary Dragonlance and Ispin's signature green shield!

Recognizing that a frontal assault on the main host was suicide, Darrett coordinated with Cudgel to create a massive, roaring military diversion along the eastern ridge, drawing the bulk of the Dragon Army's forces away from the canyon floor. With the line temporarily broken, the party slipped through the trenches, located a hidden subterranean tunnel cutting through the rock face, and officially breached the perimeter of the lost city.

docs.title: Session 18: The Path of Memories
docs.text:
Date: November 9, 1978

The party officially crossed the threshold into the ancient city ruins, entering a historical corridor known as the Path of Memories from a subterranean sector dubbed Wind’s End. The atmosphere inside was deeply oppressive; you found yourselves navigating a dark, winding stone tunnel echoing with the terrifying, hissing spiritual echoes of long-dead dragons. The supernatural dread took a heavy toll on the party's coordination, causing a sudden structural collapse that temporarily split the party into two separate groups.

Navigating the dark labyrinth, your progress was completely blocked by a massive, flooded stone passage. Utilizing the volatile orange crystals you had harvested from the Sunward Fortress, the party engineered an improvised demolition charge, successfully blowing the stone blockage to splinters and draining the chamber.

As you pressed forward through the rubble, the ancient defenses of the lost city activated. A towering, ancient Stone Golem roared to life from its pedestal, backed by a vicious, extraplanar Green Slaad that materialized from the shadows. A brutal, tactical skirmish ensued within the confined corridor, forcing the party to expend significant magical resources to shatter the golem and banish the chaotic frog-beast. Your trial was capped by a terrifying confrontation with a skeletal Death Dragon that lunged from a deep chasm, forcing the split party to fight with absolute desperation to collapse the undead horror back into the dust.

docs.title: Session 19: The Reforging of Legends
docs.text:
Date: January 25, 2026

Emerging from the dark tunnels into the majestic, ruined interior of the city, the party crossed paths with Demelin, an ancient, powerful elven archmage who had magically survived the passing of centuries. She sat with the group and shared the deep, tragic history of Onyari, explaining that it was originally a legendary flying city of pure pleasure built directly atop the conquered remains of dead dragons. She revealed that the historical Knight Sarlamir was originally dispatched to solve the city's corrupt magical imbalance, but his pride and ultimate disobedience during the Cataclysm had inadvertently made the curse infinitely worse. At the conclusion of her lecture, she presented the party with an updated copy of the Rough Guide to help navigate the ruins.

The party directed their steps toward the grand, crumbling Temple of Paladine. As you navigated the overgrown courtyard, your path was blocked by a massive, corrupted Treant that had been driven mad by the valley's lingering dark magic. The party executed a swift, coordinated skirmish, utilizing fire magic to pacify the wooden titan.

Entering the holy inner sanctum, you located the ancient, sacred forge of the temple. Utilizing your recovered spearhead and divine resources, the party conducted a holy ritual, successfully reforging the legendary Dragonlance to its absolute apex of divine power!

With the legendary weapon shining bright, the party embarked on a critical reconnaissance mission through the occupied sectors of the city. Spotting a heavy military supply line, you utilized your explosive resources to completely destroy a massive stone bridge, effectively crippling the Dragon Army's local logistics. Following up on the disruption, the party crept through the ruined streets, launching a stealth assault on a grand, occupied mansion that was currently serving as a local command outpost for the enemy leadership.

docs.title: Session 20: The Threshold of the Heavens
docs.text:
Date: March 22, 2026

The party breached the grand perimeter doors, launching a total, surprise assault on the occupied mansion within the City of Lost Names. A fierce, bloody room-to-room urban battle erupted through the grand corridors. The party fought with absolute military precision, systematically cutting through multiple squads of disciplined Dragon Army soldiers and high-ranking Draconian officers. During a sweep of the ground floor, you located an ornately carved chest; after successfully bypassing a lethal needle trap woven into the lock, you looted a substantial horde of rare coins and valuable historical jewelry.

The party then advanced up the grand staircase to breach the upper master quarters. There you cornered and engaged the ruthless enemy commander, Hask. A fast and lethal boss battle ensued, but the party's overwhelming combat synergy rapidly brought the warlord crashing down. Thoroughly searching his corpse and private desk, you struck gold: you recovered a massive bundle of highly sensitive military dispatches and tactical maps, gaining invaluable intelligence regarding the movements of the Dragon Army's supreme leadership, specifically detailing the upcoming operations of Belephaion and Lozehet.

Armed with this definitive intelligence and with the legendary Dragonlance slung over your shoulder, the party exited the mansion and marched directly toward the grand, central focal point of the entire flying city: the awe-inspiring, magical monolith known as the Threshold of the Heavens.

docs.title: Currently known quests
docs.text:
• Angajuk’s Bell quest (a whale with a boat on its back)
  • Goblin scavengers that prowl the tundra
  • A secret buried tower that was connected to Dzaan, a wizard who was burned at the stake
  • Two feuding goliath clans at the Spine of the World
  • Xardorok's Fortress

docs.title: Bryn Shander
docs.text:
Vanera, Guldrak, Mael, Aero and Ash (me) arrived together in Bryn Shander, one of the ten northern towns, after a long and arduous trip. Very quickly they learnt a bit more about Auril, the Frostmaiden, who had tightened her icy grip over the northern lands. People often made sacrifices to Auril, to keep her pleased and to ensure that things would not get any worse.

Apparently there had been multiple murders recently in the area: a dwarf in Bryn Shander, a halfling in Targos and a human in Easthaven. Each of these murders had been committed with an ice shard as a weapon. Somebody called Sephek Kaltro was implicated, a man who apparently works as a bodyguard for somebody called Targas. Sephek apparently seemed very comfortable in the cold, so the fact that the murders were committed with ice shards would seem to link them to him.

The local tavern in Bryn is called "The North Look" and many strange encounters can be had there. The tavern features a knucklehead trout that is attached to the wall called Ol' Bitey. Outside the tavern the party was approached by three people (Ailsa, Olaf and ???) with a quest to retrieve a sled. The sled had been stolen by goblins and apparently contained ingots and somehow a group of yetis was also implicated.

docs.title: Bremen
docs.text:
The party then headed towards Bremen (why?) to follow up on some leads. Bremen famously lacks any real comfort, the only real place of interest being the local taverns (five of them), one of which is called the Grumpy Moose. Here the party learnt a bit more about the everlasting winter of Auril, an "evil" wizard caught in Easthaven and a monster which is said to live in the lake of Maer Dualdon.

The party followed further leads and ended up fighting goblins that had commandeered a sled pulled by polar bears. The goblins were killed and the bears escaped, but the party had to retreat when Sephek attacked and the group proved too weak for him. Afterwards the group returned to Bremen.

docs.title: Lake Maer Dualdon
docs.text: After talking to the mayor, we went for a fishing trip on lake Maer Dualdon and heard even more rumours from somebody called Tali about a "monster" in the lake. We even caught some fish! We also met Grinsk, a fisher who promised to pay us for anything we catch.**

docs.title: Lake Maer Dualdon
docs.text:
Whilst fishing on the lake, suddenly the lake started rumbling but after a short moment the rumbling stopped again. A small boat approached the party, a rock gnome called Zorin hailed the group as the boat got closer. Shortly afterwards another boat approached us carrying a flag of Targos, the boat then told us to stop fishing (but we didn't stop). Soon the water started rumbling again and a fully-fledged pleisiosaur appeared from under the surface and ate Aero! Zorin decided he liked us and asked whether he could join the party.

After heading back to shore, Grinsk paid us for the fish we caught and Tali was very curious about our encounter with the pleisiosaur.

docs.title: Bremen
docs.text: The group headed back to Bremen to the Grumpy Moose, where Zorin gave some fish to the innkeeper to make some stew. After heading back to the campsite where we fought the goblins, we found some tracks that we were then able to follow and which lead us to Targos.

docs.title: Targos
docs.text:
Once we arrived in Targos, we headed to the local inn called the Luskan Arms, where innkeeper Owen keeps a tight ship. We spoke to town speaker Naerth who apparently had some flying snakes that could be sent out to warn about murdered Sephek.

Outside, a dog appeared which wanted us to follow it to a house, where a man called Kaegan then opened the door when we knocked. Kaegan told us that the dog was called Boy and that the dog belongs to a man named Gareth who led a party up Kelvin's Cairn about three days ago. I cast speak with animals on boy and the dog was able to tell me about avalanches that had occurred while climbing up the cairn and that Gareth had also been attacked. We decided to follow up on this lead and headed off towards Caer-Konig with boy as it is the place that is closest to the cairn.

docs.title: Caer-Konig
docs.text: Once we arrived in Caer-Konig, we managed to acquire some climbing gear, which we definitely would need to climb up the cairn. Before setting off, we had a look around the village: it had an inn called the Northern Light and a tavern called the Hook, Line and Sinker.

docs.title: Kelvin's Cairn
docs.text: With our climbing gear, we headed up the cairn until we found a few more dogs. These dogs were again able to tell us a bit more (via speak with animals) and we learnt that Gareth had been ambushed by a yeti. We were attacked by some crag cats, after dispersing those we headed up the mountain until we came to a cave with tracks of blood outside.

docs.title: Kelvin's Cairn
docs.text:
After entering the cave, we managed to locate a yeti female and yeti infant inside. One of the party members called Fishfinger was also being kept in the cave. Once Fishfinger had been located, a large male yeti came back into the cave, but I managed to deter him from attacking us as I do speak yeti language. In exchange for leaving, we promised not to hurt the yeti female and infant.

After leaving the cave, we headed further up the cairn and found two more of the party frozen to death. We also managed to rescue Gareth (how??) and returned back to base camp and then to Caer-Konig.

docs.title: Caer-Konig
docs.text: Back in town, we accompanied Gareth back to the Northern Light. We met Ally and Cory, who run the place. Apparently there had been some thefts recently, with goats, pearls and a magical lantern having gone missing. We quickly managed to recover the goats and the pearls by following tracks behind the inn, but could not find the lantern. More investigation seemed to suggest it might have been stolen and taken to a local duergar stronghold, so we set off towards it.

docs.title: Duergar Stronghold
docs.text: Once we arrived at the stronghold, we slowly made our way inside and encountered quite a bit of resistance from angry duergar dwarves.

docs.title: Duergar Stronghold
docs.text:
The party cleared out the duergar outpost which was led by Nildar Sunblight, a duergar thief. Mael became the proud owner of a bucket while the others found the goods that were stolen from Caer-Konig, 2 goats, some beads and a magical colour changing lantern.

You also found a note from a certain Durth, who turned out to be Nildar's brother, in which they mentioned collecting chardalyn.

Chardalyn turned out to be a strong, crystal-like material which becomes as malleable as clay when treated with the proper tools and knowledge.

docs.title: Easthaven
docs.text:
Durth mentioned he had set up camp on the Easthaven ferry. (The lake has been frozen shut for weeks so the ferry is no longer in operation.)

Afterwards the party headed towards Easthaven because you had heard a wizard would be burned at the stake. All of you watched the poor man being roasted alive and while doing so, you were approached by Captain Arlaggath, the chief of the town's militia, who asked you to lead the search for 4 lost fishermen. When you were about to discuss the reward, Mael told the captain about Durth's note and town speaker Waylen (from Easthaven) was informed at once. He asked you to clear the ferry of any bandits that were present. You were given a kill licence.

docs.title: Easthaven ferry
docs.text:
Ash shapeshifted into a horse, after which the horsing around promptly stopped and Durth Sunblight received the beating of a lifetime: Mael unleashed a thunderwave, Ash kicked away and, to add insult to injury, Vanera ferociously intimidated him after which he spilled the beans and told the party that his father, Xardorok Sunblight, has a secret castle in the mountains in which he is creating a chardalyn dragon with the intent of destroying all of Ten Towns.

Mael then decided to violate about 80% of the Geneva Convention by submitting Durth to the harshest prison regime imaginable, including being ball gagged and being hosed down twice on a daily basis.

docs.title: Cauldron caves
docs.text: Captain Arlaggath gently urged you to go looking for the four missing fishermen which led you to a series of encounters in the cauldron caves on Lac Dinneshere: you encountered a water weird, an ice giant skeleton and Maud Chiselbone herself, a sea hag. You overpowered Maud who left you her cauldron of plenty (a magical object which feed a small village). For a change, you decided not to spare Maud's life as Mael stabbed her through the throat.

docs.title: Easthaven
docs.text:
After having dispatched with Maud and her crazy soup cauldron, we headed back to Easthaven. Before heading back to the captain, we headed to the White Lady Inn to have some rest. The owner was a bard named Rinaldo, who apparently also organises seances to speak with “The Lady of the Lake. We obviously wanted to take part in such a seance, during which we learnt that there is evil in the lake, but also that evil cultists had taken over a castle in Caer-Dineval. After the seance, we managed to get a good night’s rest.

The next day we headed to captain Arlaggath with the cauldron, and we informed him that we had not been able to locate any survivors in the cave. As a reward he gave us a bag of tricks! The captain suggested that the town speaker might be interested in purchasing the cauldron from us.

Town speaker Waylen wanted the cauldron and offered us one gemstone worth 500GP each for it. At that moment he unfortunately only had two gemstones but he told us to come back in 48 hours for the rest; we left the cauldron with him and then headed off to find the cultists.

docs.title: Caer-Dineval
docs.text:
The village had both a tavern and an inn, however the inn (Diney’s Rest) had been closed for a very long time. We headed up the hill towards the tavern called The Uphill Climb.

Inside we found the owner, a man called Roark. He was a bit reluctant to talk to us at first, but after pushing him a bit we heard that the inn had not been doing very well at all lately. Apparently people in black robes had been coming and going to the castle.

Ash decides to check out the castle a bit first to get a better idea of what is going on. She shape shifts into a mouse and scurries around and gets a general idea of the layout, along with an idea of how many cultists there are. Once back, the party comes up with a ploy to capture a couple of cultists and interrogate them to get some more information. From that we learnt that the cultists are a group that call themselves the Knights of the Black Sword, led by somebody called Levistus.

The party then took the captured cultists' clothes to disguise themselves, Apparently the cultists also wear some kind of Amulet of the Black Sword which Vanera tried on but it made them feel rather strange.

Zorin and Ash made Guldrak and Mael invisible. Zorin and Vanera then disguised themselves whilst Ash shapeshifted into a horse that Zorin could ride. The plot worked and we made it into the castle! Once inside we then managed to kill about 12 of the cultists. Next up: Levistus!

docs.title: 🐋 Session 7: Voyage of the Angajuk
docs.text:
10/01/2026

Zorin ended the previous battle by cutting off one the cultist’s pinky ‘out of curiosity’.

You went inside of the castle and met Mere who was a mere servant and not the sharpest tool in the shed. He told you the lay-out of the castle after which you decided to give a knock on Kadroth’s door. Kadroth knocked back but was quickly overpowered with the help of a magic boar. He turned out to be a bully towards Mere and he was also the leader of the Black Swords Cult. You offered Mere a chance at revenge in which he burned Kadroth with a glowing red poker. You then took Kadroth to the town speaker’s magnificent bedroom and he had no interest in Kadroth, he just wanted the cultist’s gone out of his castle as soon as possible.

Kadroth was then executed by Mere with the mere stab of a dagger but not before Kadroth had offered you all lifetime memberships of the Black Swords cult in the form of small chardalyn amulets. Speaker Crannock Siever was happy that the cultists were gone and told you that the party was always welcome. You also arranged for Mere to be permanently employed at the castle. Upstairs you admired the beautifully decorated bedroom of the town speaker and you also met Touché, Kadroth’s cat. She gave you some more info about the castle, its inhabitants and Kadroth’s behaviour.

You also encountered Hethyl, the cult’s soothsayer who had foreseen your arrival and knew her end was near. She shared information with you on Xardorok’s plans, namely that he's about to unleash a terrible doom upon Ten-Towns since he wants to become the first above-ground duergar ruler. She warned you not to face Xardorok too soon but to become even more powerful by exploring the outreaches of Icewind Dale. She also informed you that Xardorok’s forge is powered by the beating heart of a red dragon. She told you that the Black Swords hate the duergar and at the end of her message she died peacefully (with the help of Mael who had been opening all of the windows).

You then went on to explore the subterranean level of the castle, mainly because of rumours of delicious hams that were stored there and also because of some iced popsicles. In the catacombs you encountered Avarice who was part of the Blacks Swords Cult yet seemed to keep her distance from Kadroth and she also seemed to be pursuing other interests which she refused to share with you. You struck a deal with her and allowed her to keep her living quarters underneath the castle and she agreed to help you when needed in defeating the duergar and thwarting their plans.

You all became level 4 characters once you came out of the catacombs.

docs.title: 🔮 Session 8: The Caves of Hunger
docs.text:
29/03/2026

As level 4 characters you noticed that people are way more talkative and you were able to eavesdrop on some interesting stories:

  • Angajuk’s Bell quest (whale with boat on back)
  • A comet falling from the sky about a week ago, people are having difficulty sleeping because of a buzz they are hearing
  • Goblin scavengers prowl on the tundra
  • A secret buried tower that was connected to Dzaan, the wizard who was burned at the stake
  • Two feuding goliath clans at the Spine of the World

After leaving Caer Dineval you went back to Easthaven for another séance with Rinaldo and the white lady of the lake. Rinaldo was happy to see you back and shared some info on what was going on in the Ten Towns region. The lady of the lake didn’t share that much information with you and you decided to head to Termalaine to clear out a mine which had been overrun by kobolds.

The mine proved less eventful than expected: most of the kobolds in the mine wanted to be left alone and the one who wanted to fight turned out to be possessed by a ghost: the ghost of a deceased botanist who wanted to complete his life’s work. Zorin snatched his satchel, which was then stolen from Ash by Termalaine’s town speaker who hasn’t been seen since. Mael discovered a psi crystal after knocking down a weird skull in the mine and has started hearing some kind of repetitive signal sent out by some kind of beacon.

At the end you decided to wait for Sephek and Torga. When they returned with the dog sled you ambushed them. Sephek wasn’t easy to overcome yet you ended up victorious. You were planning on bringing Sephek’s body to Bryn Shander.

docs.title: Bryn Shander
docs.text:
We headed to Bryn Shander with Sephek Kaltro's body. We meet Lynn Trollbane at the inn with the body. She rewards us with gold, silver and gemstones.

Some people approach us who have been suffering from a ringing noise in their heads for more than a week. Nothing obvious in common between them. Mael can sense the direction of the source, somewhere in the south towards the Spine of the World.

docs.title: Spine of the World
docs.text:
As we get closer we encounter two large worms, which Ash convinces to not attack us. The worms then escort us to the "ship", which is some kind of monstrosity which has crashed there. We enter the ship where there are chains and manacles designed to lock up creatures. There are four creatures roaming around inside the ship, each a bit stranger than the next.

We head upstairs to see somebody called Voryn, a sort of squid creature. He is happy that the "beacon" is being picked up and that it has led us to him. He's been broadcasting it for more than a week as an SOS. Apparently a psi-crystal would help fix the ship, Mael gave him the crystal that we found in the mine previously. We hang around a bit while he installs it to fix the "warp core". He gives us some kind of gun, which Mael takes. We find another gun in a crate, which Mael also takes. Voryn finds a pacifier rod.

After a while, Voryn comes back and tells us that he was able to fix the ship and they can leave again. They take off and head back into outer space.

As we head off, we see a monster burst out of the snow and head towards us. We fight! Ash summons a Giant Elk which stays with us for the rest of the day. The bulette is defeated.

docs.title: Tundra
docs.text:
We head towards the location where goblins have been reported to have been raiding from the tundra. As we get closer we notice trails and footprints, which we follow. We encounter two watch towers and Mael uses one of the weapons from Voryn on the tower, a lot of screaming erupts and then the sound of a horn being blown. Fight!

We kill the first four goblins and then we get the elk to ram the tower. Ash casts thunder wave on one of the towers and causes it to partly collapse. We kill all the goblins and next time we intend to enter the fortress.

docs.title: Phandalin Campaign Dossier: The Lost Mine of Phandelver
Notable Persons & Factions
docs.text:
• Gundren Rockseeker: A hopeful dwarven entrepreneur who, along with his brothers, discovered the long-lost entrance to Wave Echo Cave. His kidnapping serves as the anchor for the party's contract.
  • Sildar Hallwinter: A distinguished member of the Lords' Alliance who was ambushed alongside Gundren. After his rescue, he establishes a temporary headquarters in Phandalin to investigate local disturbances.
  • The Redbrands: A ruthless, mercenary bandit gang wearing distinctive scarlet cloaks, who have spent months terrorizing Phandalin from their hidden headquarters beneath Tresendar Manor.
  • Iarno "Glassstaff" Albrek: A former wizard of the Lords' Alliance who went rogue, adopting the moniker "Glassstaff" due to his signature weapon. He took command of the Redbrands under orders from a higher master.
  • The Black Spider (Nezznar): A mysterious, calculating villain operating from the shadows of the valley, utilizing an army of goblins, dopplegängers, and bugbears to seize control of Wave Echo Cave.

docs.title: Lost Relics & Locations
docs.text:
• Wave Echo Cave: A legendary subterranean cavern housing the ancient Forge of Spells, a mystical site capable of imbuing mundane weapons with permanent magical properties.
