  • Thundertree: A ruined, ash-choked village situated near the borders of Neverwinter Wood, currently overrun by toxic plant monsters, zombies, and a territorial young green dragon.
  • Cragmaw Castle: A ruined, multi-towered stone stronghold hidden deep within the brush of Neverwinter Wood, serving as the throne room for the goblinoid chieftain King Grol.

docs.title: The Black Spider's Decrees
docs.text:
During your journey across the Triboar Trail, the Black Spider projected a magical psychic ultimatum to the party:

"So, finally we meet. For the first time and unfortunately for you this will also be the very last. You must be the gang of outlaws who is trying to stop me from claiming my rightful ownership of Wave Echo Cave. I can tell you that your efforts will be absolutely worthless: nothing will stop me from taking over Phandalin and the riches that the mine can provide. Thanks to your 'friend' Gundren I now have been able to locate the entrance of Wave Echo Cave and will soon be heading into the mine to claim what is rightfully mine. My minion Glasstaff was supposed to put a halt to your efforts in the Redbrand hideout but he fled like a coward... now he has the opportunity to redeem himself by sending me your heads after this encounter. I bid you farewell... you have been most unworthy opponents."

docs.title: Session 1: Ambush on the Triboar Trail
docs.text:
The campaign began with a frantic clash of steel along the high road. While escorting a supply wagon down the trail toward the frontier settlement of Phandalin, the party was suddenly ambushed by a coordinated raiding party of Cragmaw goblins. Springing into a defensive formation, you fought back fiercely, driving your blades through three of the raiders while successfully pinning and capturing a lone goblin named Grom.

With the immediate threat neutralized, you secured the captured goblin, gathered the scattered reins, and successfully escorted the supply cart into Phandalin. Your first stop was Barthen's Provisions, where you officially delivered the much-needed crates and supplies to the grateful storekeeper. Seeking a secure place to base your operations, you booked rooms at the local Stonehill Inn and dragged the bound Grom down into the town's makeshift dungeon cells for overnight confinement.

The following morning, the party mobilized and traveled back to the original ambush site to search for tracks. Using a combination of stern threats and promises of survival, you coerced Grom into acting as a scout. The terrified goblin capitulated, leading the party through a series of hidden wilderness trails directly to the camouflaged mouth of the notorious Cragmaw Hideout.

docs.title: Session 2: The Cragmaw Hideout
docs.text:
The party cautiously crossed the threshold into the damp caverns of the Cragmaw Hideout, immediately running into a pack of snarling, half-starved wolves chained near the entrance. Seeking to use their captive as a meat shield, the party attempted to aggressively push Grom further down the narrow passage to trigger any hidden traps. However, the clever goblin seized the moment and shrieked at the top of his lungs, successfully alerting the interior sentries. Reacting instantly, the goblins pulled a set of wooden levers, unleashing a massive, roaring wall of dammed water down the tunnel that violently washed the party out of the cave mouth.

Undeterred by the muddy setback, you gathered yourselves, drew your weapons, and marched right back into the cavern. Pushing into a smoke-filled side chamber, you caught a large squad of goblins completely off-guard while they were distracted eating their rations. The party launched a total assault, slaughtering the raiders to a man. Moving deeper into the complex, you confronted a goblin sub-boss named Yeemik; you swiftly executed him and rescued Sildar Hallwinter, who was being held hostage on a high ledge. To save Sildar from the indignity of traveling naked, Kevin quickly tailored a remarkably creative, makeshift suit out of salvaged goblin garments. Armed with a looted scimitar, a grateful Sildar officially joined your ranks.

The party then crossed a natural stone bridge to infiltrate the twin pool cavern, cutting through successive waves of goblin archers. As you entered the final cavern, Ulvor was completely blindsided by the massive bugbear chieftain, Klarg, and nearly perished under the brute's heavy morningstar. However, the party rallied instantly: Klarg’s vicious pet wolf, Ripper, was rapidly put down by the frontline fighters, followed immediately by Wilbin unleashing a perfectly placed acid dart that one-shot Klarg, melting the bugbear chieftain where he stood. Searching the cave's storage crates, you discovered a mountain of stolen merchant supplies stamped with a prominent Blue Lion sigil. Prying open one of the reinforced chests, the party discovered and claimed three magnificent suits of pristine chain mail armor.

docs.title: Session 3: The Rumors of Phandalin
docs.text:
The party made a triumphant return to Phandalin, booking comfortable rooms at the Stonehill Inn to rest and gather local intelligence. Over flagons of ale, the tavern patrons shared a wealth of troubling rumors regarding the region, detailing the suspicious activities of Sister Garaele at the local shrine, a rising threat of Orc Raiders along the frontier, and the brutal murder of a local woodcarver named Thel Dendrar. You also spoke with a young boy named Pip, who shared a secret about a hidden path in the woods, and learned of the Lionshield Coster merchant cartel.

The next morning, you visited the Lionshield Coster exchange building, presenting the merchant mistress with the recovered Blue Lion crates and receiving a handsome gold reward for your honesty. From there, you paid a visit to the Townmaster’s hall to review the local bounties. You found that Sildar Hallwinter had officially established a temporary command office within the building, actively launching an investigation into the mysterious disappearance of a prominent Lords' Alliance wizard named Iarno Albrek.

Your next stop was the town shrine, where Sister Garaele officially contracted the party to locate the legendary banshee Agatha, presenting you with a specific magical quest to extract crucial information from the spirit. Seeking to test the local opposition, the party marched down to the rowdy Sleeping Giant taproom, a notorious hangout for the scarlet-cloaked bandit gang known as the Redbrands. A brief, violent barroom brawl erupted. You successfully knocked out three Redbrand ruffians, dragging the final surviving thug into an alley until he terrifiedly spilled his secrets.

The captive revealed that the gang operated under the absolute command of a mysterious, staff-wielding wizard known as "Glassstaff." He admitted that the murdered woodcarver Thel Dendrar’s body had been dragged down to their lair, and his grieving wife and children had been locked away in iron cages as slaves. Armed with the precise location of the hideout, the party marched out to the perimeter of Tresendar Manor, utilizing Pip's secret woodland path to discover a dark, overgrown entrance leading directly into the underdark tunnels.

docs.title: Session 4: Infiltration of Tresendar Manor
docs.text:
Utilizing the secret forest entrance that young Pip had revealed, the party slipped quietly into the damp, subterranean vaults stretching beneath the ruins of Tresendar Manor. The moment you stepped into the natural cavern rift, you could hear the eerie, echoing telepathic whispers of a bizarre creature you dubbed the "Eye Monster." Rather than letting the telepathic aberration pin you down, the party pushed forward into the adjacent guardrooms, ambushing a squad of brutish bugbears and slaying them before they could sound the alarm.

Advancing down a stone corridor, you threw open a heavy oak door to find a group of Redbrand ruffians completely distracted by a high-stakes card game. The party capitalized on the surprise, cutting down the bandits at the table before they could reach for their weapons. Wishing to ensure their rear flank was secure, you doubled back to systematically purge the southeastern wing of the complex.

The dungeon crawling proved highly treacherous; while navigating a dark hallway, multiple party members triggered a pressure plate and tumbled into a deep, concealed pit trap. At the bottom of the pit, a group of ancient skeletons rattled to life to attack. The party fought back from the dirt, smashing the bones to splinters and discovering three valuable magical rings buried in the debris. After climbing out of the pit, you advanced on the holding cells, cutting through the final line of Redbrand jailers who were guarding the heavy iron cages where the town prisoners were weeping.

docs.title: Session 5: Secrets of the Redbrands
docs.text:
The party successfully shattered the iron padlocks on the holding cells, rescuing Mirna Dendrar and her terrified children from their grim confinement. In an act of profound respect, you also located and recovered the skeletal remains of her murdered husband, Thel, to ensure he received a proper burial. Overwhelmed with gratitude, Mirna pulled the party aside and shared a valuable family secret, providing precise coordinates to a priceless emerald heirloom hidden within the ruined wilderness village of Thundertree, located far to the east of Neverwinter.

With the civilians escorted to safety, the party completed a systematic purge of the remaining chambers, ensuring that the Redbrand gang would never terrorize the citizens of Phandalin ever again. During a brief scouting run into the surrounding woods, Kaz spotted a magnificent wild falcon; using patience and a gentle touch, he successfully befriended the bird of prey, naming it Bloodwing.

Returning to the central rift, you decided to feed the ravenous Nothic by tossing several fresh Redbrand corpses down into its chasm. Delighted by the gory feast, the monster shared deep secrets via telepathy, revealing that "Glassstaff" was merely a cover identity for the missing Lords' Alliance wizard, Iarno Albrek. Satisfied with the intelligence, you honored your bargain and allowed the aberration to depart the caverns in peace.

A meticulous search of Glassstaff’s private laboratory and writing desk yielded a trove of damning evidence, including a direct, handwritten letter addressed to Iarno from a mysterious criminal mastermind signing off as the "Black Spider." With the hideout entirely broken, the party marched back out into the sunlight, finalizing their tactical plans to travel northeast to locate the banshee Agatha.

docs.title: Session 6: The Orkish Masquerade
docs.text:
The party kicked off the morning by purchasing a sturdy supply cart and a pair of horses from the Phandalin stables. You loaded your gear and traveled northeast along the high Triboar Trail. Along the road, you intercepted a goblin scouting party; after a brief skirmish, you successfully extracted the precise, hidden location of Cragmaw Castle, learning it sat nestled deep within the southern edge of the dense Neverwinter Wood.

Continuing along the trail, you arrived at the haunted, overgrown clearing of Conyberry and invoked the ritual to summon Agatha the Banshee. As the terrifying spirit materialized from the mist, Gillian stepped forward and confidently used the party's singular question to ask: "Am I the most beautiful of them all?" Flattered or amused by the sheer audacity of the bard, the spirit provided her answers before vanishing back into the ether. The party then established a secure camp within the ruins of Conyberry to secure a long rest.

The next morning, you steered the cart south toward the rocky bluffs of Wyvern Tor to hunt down a disruptive band of Orc Raiders. Upon locating the dark mouth of their mountain cave, the party chose to execute a brilliant, high-stakes tactical deception rather than a frontal assault. Gillian utilized his magic to disguise himself as a hulking orc warrior, while Ulvor dirtied his armor to follow closely behind acting as Gillian's miserable "servant." Approaching the lone orc sentry guarding the threshold, Gillian delivered an incredibly convincing vocal performance, completely fooling the guard and commanding the gullible orc to turn around and happily lead them directly inside the main chieftain's cavern.

docs.title: Session 7: The Ambush on the Trail
docs.text:
The orkish masquerade dissolved into absolute violence the moment you stepped into the inner sanctum. The party launched a devastating surprise assault, slaying the orc chieftain Brughor Axebiter and his towering pet ogre, Gog. You severed the raiders' heads to serve as proof of the deed and traveled safely back to Conyberry for a brief rest before steering the horse cart back down the Triboar Trail toward Phandalin.

However, mid-way through the journey, the forest went completely silent as a high-stakes ambush was sprung. Stepping out from the treeline to block the road were Glassstaff and the legendary mastermind himself, the Black Spider. The dross villain raised his hand, delivering a chilling psychic message directly into your minds, gloating that your efforts to stop him from claiming Wave Echo Cave were entirely worthless and that his minions would collect your heads. The moment the monologue concluded, the Black Spider swigged a magical potion of invisibility and completely vanished from the battlefield.

A frantic, deadly skirmish erupted in the center of the road. Glassstaff unleashed a barrage of arcane missiles while backed by three massive, chitinous giant spiders that lunged at the horses. The party fought with exceptional coordination, crushing the giant arachnids and successfully subduing Glassstaff before he could escape a second time. Searching the defeated wizard, Gillian proudly claimed his signature glass staff as a prize of war. Through this monumental victory over the campaign's primary antagonists, the party's growing capabilities reached an elite threshold, and you officially attained level 4!

You escorted the horse cart safely back into Phandalin. Your first stop was Sister Garaele’s shrine, but the priestess was deeply annoyed to find that Gillian had wasted her magical question on personal vanity instead of asking about the legendary spellbook; she presented the party with a cold shoulder and dismissed you. Undeterred, you marched to the Townmaster’s hall to successfully claim the heavy gold bounty on the Wyvern Tor orcs. Sildar Hallwinter warmly congratulated the group on defeating Glassstaff, but strongly urged you to immediate prioritize finding Cragmaw Castle to rescue the kidnapped Gundren Rockseeker.

docs.title: Session 8: The Ruined Village of Thundertree
docs.text:
After restocking your packs with fresh rations and adventuring gear in Phandalin, the party traveled north to the great coastal city of Neverwinter. You spent the evening resting inside a serene local monastery, reuniting with various friends and family members before heading down to a local tavern to get thoroughly drunk. The following morning, you shook off the alcohol and marched east along the river basin toward the ash-choked ruins of Thundertree.

Upon crossing the threshold of the overgrown village, you were met by a weathered druid named Reidoth. He delivered a stern warning regarding the local landscape, noting that the ruins were currently crawling with hostile twig blights, giant spiders, and irradiated ash zombies. He passionately requested the party's assistance in driving off a dangerous young green dragon that had recently claimed the town's central stone tower as its personal roost.

The party systematically swept through the ruined cottages, clearing out pockets of monsters. While searching the collapsed remains of a wealthy manor, you successfully uncovered the lost emerald heirloom necklace that Mirna Dendrar had told you about. The exploration took a terrifying turn when a massive Giant Spider lunged from the rafters to attack Kevin; the sheer horror of the arachnid's bite caused Kevin to suffer a severe emotional breakdown in the middle of the combat. After stabilizing him, you pushed into a fortified structure and discovered a coven of dark dragon cultists. Driven by sudden fury, Kevin launched an immediate, unprovoked assault on their lines, sending the cultists screaming out into the open streets as a frantic skirmish began.

docs.title: Session 9: The Cult of the Dragon
docs.text:
The urban skirmish through the streets of Thundertree was swift and uncompromising. The party cut off all avenues of escape, successfully executing every single member of the Dragon Cult. Searching the corpse of their fanatical leader, Favric, you hit a massive tactical jackpot: you uncovered a locked iron coffer containing a magnificent tribute intended for the dragon, which held three flawless diamonds worth one hundred gold pieces each along with a magical potion of flying.

With the cult broken, the party completed a total purge of the remaining sectors of the village, hacking through clusters of aggressive Twig Blights and putting down the shuffling Ash Zombies. To mark your victory and clear the sightlines, the party gathered around the central square and used their combined strength to forcefully push over the massive weathered stone statue standing in the plaza.

The group returned to Reidoth’s fortified cottage to secure a well-deserved rest. Over a camp dinner, you discussed advanced draconic tactics with the druid, preparing your strategy to confront the green beast. The next morning, you marched up the hill to the base of the grand stone tower. Peeking through the shattered masonry, you spotted the massive, scaled form of Venomfang pretending to be fast asleep amidst his gold hoard. Refusing to waste time on diplomacy, Ulvor drew his weapons and immediately charged headfirst into the tower to attack.

docs.title: Session 10: The Fall of King Grol
docs.text:
The confrontation inside the stone tower was an absolute crucible of fire and poison. Venomfang reared back and unleashed a devastating torrent of toxic poison breath across the chamber, instantly knocking Kevin unconscious and leaving him bleeding out on the floor. The party rallied with desperate heroism; while the frontline fighters traded brutal blows with the green dragon, the spellcasters administered clutch healing magic to stabilize Kevin. Through an incredible display of martial violence, you broke the dragon's resolve, dealing a fatal blow that sent Venomfang crashing down dead amidst his hoard.

A profoundly grateful Reidoth thanked the party for saving the region from the draconic shadow, fulfilling his bargain by guiding the taskforce through a series of secret forest trails directly to the gates of Cragmaw Castle. After bidding the druid farewell, the party approached the brooding stone fortress. Creeping up to a reinforced side door, you jammed a crowbar into the seam and used brute physical force to pop the iron hinges.

The party flooded into the castle corridors, launching a swift assault that cut through a defensive line of surprised Hobgoblins and goblin sentries. You methodically breached the central chambers, throwing open the doors to the high throne room to find a chaotic scene: the massive bugbear King Grol was in the middle of a tense negotiation with a disguised dopplegänger shapeshifter, while the unconscious, battered body of Gundren Rockseeker lay draped across the floorboards. A fast, brutal melee erupted. The party cut down King Grol and executed the shapeshifter before it could escape. Ulvor rushed to the center of the room, uncorking a potent healing potion and pouring it down Gundren's throat, successfully reviving the dwarven merchant as he leaned shakily against the castle wall surrounded by the corpses of his captors.

docs.title: Session 11: The Map to Wave Echo Cave
docs.text:
With Gundren Rockseeker safely breathing and resting against the throne room wall, the party set out to systematically clear the remaining wings of Cragmaw Castle. While exploring a reinforced northern tower, you accidentally breached a high-security cell and were instantly attacked by a roaring, ferocious Owlbear that had been locked away by the goblins; the party formed a tight defensive line and successfully put the monster down. Returning to the empty throne room, you conducted a thorough search of King Grol's filthy bedframe, successfully locating Gundren's highly coveted, handwritten map hidden deep inside the mattress padding.

Disaster struck as the party retraced their steps through the grand main hall. Wilbin misstepped and accidentally triggered a hidden mechanical pressure plate, setting off a loud, clanging alarm trap that echoed through the entire fortress structure. The trap instantly alerted the remaining garrison; dozens of armed Goblins and disciplined Hobgoblins came streaming out of the barracks like a tidal wave to surround the party. A fierce, bottleneck battle erupted in the center of the hall, but the party held their ground, painting the stone floors crimson until the entire goblinoid force was completely eradicated.

With Cragmaw Castle officially cleared of all evil, the party organized a secure litter to transport Gundren. You traveled back to the coast to retrieve your supply cart from Neverwinter before completing the long journey back to Phandalin. Arriving back at the Townmaster’s hall, you reunited Gundren with a deeply relieved Sildar Hallwinter. With the map recovered and the dwarf brothers reunited, both Gundren and Sildar expressed an immense, burning eagerness to immediately launch an expedition into the depths of Wave Echo Cave.

docs.title: Session 12: Infiltration of the Mine
docs.text:
The morning began with a grand celebration at the Townmaster's hall. True to his word, a weeping Gundren Rockseeker formally rewarded the party for his salvation, signing an official decree promising the taskforce a permanent 10% share of all the mine's wealth the moment the excavation operation was up and running. Before departing, the party paid a visit to Sister Garaele’s shrine, purchasing a fresh crate of potent magical potions for the dungeon crawl ahead. Later that afternoon, Gillian took center stage at the Stonehill Inn, loudly singing grand epic songs about his own beauty and heroism while the rest of the crew drank to success.

Following Gundren's map, the party traveled into the jagged mountains and located the dark, yawning mouth of Wave Echo Cave. Lighting your torches, you stepped into the ancient subterranean tunnels, immediately noticing a rhythmic, booming sound like crashing ocean waves echoing through the stone every few minutes.

The initial descent proved incredibly treacherous as the ancient security systems of the mine activated. The party was first ambushed by a slow-moving colony of corrosive Ochre Jellies that dropped from the ceiling, but you split your attacks effectively to dissolve them. Pushing deeper into the dark tunnels, you were swarmed by a pack of feral, flesh-eating Ghouls that lunged from the shadows of an old mining tracks; the frontline fighters formed a defensive wall and cut them down. Finally, as you crossed a wide cavern chasm, a massive cloud of blood-drinking Stirges swarmed out of the stalactites, but the party deployed quick area-of-effect magic to swat the monsters from the air and clear the passage.

docs.title: Session 13: The Forge of Spells
docs.text:
The party advanced deeper into the subterranean maze of Wave Echo Cave, with the mysterious, booming thunder of the cavern echoing through your chest cavity every few minutes. While exploring a southern junction, the group was ambushed by a squad of ancient skeleton archers; you rapidly closed the distance and smashed the bones to dust. Seeking to investigate a patch of strange vegetation, Wilbin casually poked a cluster of glowing mushrooms, inadvertently unleashing a cloud of poisonous spores that left him coughing and sputtering.

Turning a corner into a grand, reinforced laboratory, the party came face-to-face with an ancient guardian: a multi-eyed Spectator floating over a glowing green hearth. The aberration declared it was under eternal orders to guard the legendary Forge of Spells. Utilizing exceptional diplomacy and quick-witted deception, the party successfully convinced the extraplanar guardian that the ancient war was over and all threats had passed; satisfied that its contract was complete, the Spectator blinked its multiple eyes and peacefully de-manifested back to its home plane. Searching the glowing Brazier of Green Flame, the party uncovered two legendary magical artifacts: the glowing mace Lightbringer and the magnificent steel breastplate Dragonguard.

Your celebration was instantly cut short as a horde of shuffling zombies came spilling out of the northern tunnels, led by a terrifying, floating green skull wreathed in fire—a Flameskull. A chaotic firefight erupted. In a bizarre turn of events, the volatile Flameskull attempted to cast a high-level fireball spell within the cramped stone corridor, inadvertently caught in its own blast radius and committing spectacular accidental suicide. With the area clear, Kaz sent his faithful falcon, Bloodwing, soaring across the expanse of the Great Cavern to map out the terrain ahead.

docs.title: Session 14: The Great Cavern
docs.text:
Following Bloodwing's aerial scouting report, the party advanced into the massive, echoing expanse of the Great Cavern. The quiet was shattered as a pack of feral ghouls ambushed your flank, but you swiftly put them down with practiced steel. Moving toward a set of reinforced iron doors, the frontline fighters stacked up and violently forced the threshold open, completely catching a large squad of Bugbear occupiers off-guard and slaughtering them before they could form a defensive line.

Pushing deeper into the northern tunnels, you discovered a breathtaking subterranean lake filling a massive cavern bowl. As you mapped the shoreline, you spotted an advanced troop of Bugbears aggressively searching the rocky crevices for hidden treasure. Seizing the tactical initiative, Ulvor fearlessly leaped straight down into the rocky pit below, instantly finding himself completely surrounded by the furry brutes. A chaotic, high-stakes melee followed; despite the terrible positioning, the party provided heavy ranged support from the ledges, allowing Ulvor to paint the stones red and eventually slaughter every single bugbear in the pit.

With the immediate sector cleared of hostiles, a hilarious wave of competitive energy washed over the group. To break the tension of the dungeon crawl, Gillian and Wilbin marched over to a deep cavern chasm and engaged in a highly animated, competitive pissing competition to see who could clear the gap. Amused but practical, Alex stepped forward with his divine resources, systematically administering healing magic to patch up every single battered party member—purposefully skipping Gillian entirely to teach the bard a lesson about vanity.

docs.title: Session 15: Showdown with the Black Spider
docs.text:
The final expedition through the deep recesses of Wave Echo Cave yielded an incredible initial find: tucked away within a forgotten iron lockbox, the party discovered and claimed a pair of magnificent Gauntlets of Ogre Strength.

Weapon in hand, you breached the grand double doors leading into the magnificent central temple of the mine—and finally came face-to-face with the Nezznar, the Black Spider. The dross villain stood atop a high stone dais, flanked by a massive army of heavily armed bugbears and a trio of skittering giant spiders. Holding Gundren’s sobbing, captive brother by the hair, the Black Spider delivered a final, villainous monologue, mocking Glassstaff’s failure in Phandalin and demanding that the party immediately drop their weapons and surrender the Forge of Spells, or watch the dwarf brother die.

The party responded with a unified battle cry, launching an absolute, no-compromises final assault. The temple chamber erupted into a spectacular storm of steel, fire, and divine light. While the frontline fighters engaged the giant spiders and formed a defensive wall against the charging bugbears, the spellcasters unleashed their ultimate arcane payloads directly at the dais. Through sheer tactical brilliance and uncompromising violence, the party shattered the enemy lines, cutting down the bugbear army and permanently ending the Black Spider's reign of terror. Defeated and victorious, the party stood triumphant in the reclaimed halls of Wave Echo Cave, securing the future of Phandalin forever!

docs.title: Curse of Strahd: Campaign Lore and Reference
The Von Zarovich Lineage
docs.text:
The history of Barovia is deeply tied to the bloodline of King Barov and Queen Ravenovia. Their son, Sergei, was tragically murdered by his own brother, Strahd. This dark fratricide was driven by Strahd's obsession with Tatyana, Sergei’s betrothed. Desperate to possess her, Strahd made a horrific pact with the Dark Powers, becoming a vampire. Rather than surrender to Strahd, Tatyana threw herself to her death. Today, Barovia exists as a grim demiplane forged entirely within Strahd's twisted consciousness, trapping him in a Domain of Dread alongside any unfortunate souls who cross the borders.

Strahd believes his soul is utterly lost to evil, harboring no pity or remorse. The only event that truly haunts his hollow existence is the death of Tatyana. Because no humanity remains within him, he relentlessly hunts Ireena Kolyana—the daughter of the late burgomaster of Barovia—believing her to be the latest vessel for Tatyana’s reincarnated soul. Ireena herself is torn, entirely unsure of her destiny and caught between a strange attraction to the vampire lord and a fierce desire to protect her family.

docs.title: The Land and its Inhabitants
docs.text:
Barovia is a insular and bleak domain holding fewer than three thousand residents. Finding a suitable mate is a constant struggle for the locals, and the oppressive clouds overhead cast a stark, white, and perpetual twilight across the landscape. The Barovians have developed bizarre customs for dealing with outsiders; they often stare in eerie silence, yet they harbor violent tempers that can flare up at a moment's notice. Should a native be mistreated by an outsider, a intense sense of social cohesion unifies the community against the threat.

Strahd rules this land with absolute authority and absolute madness. When looking out from his castle spires, his angular, powerful face contorts with a tragedy darker than the night itself. He watches the arrival of new adventurers with a twisted smile, seeing them as mere playthings. He tests them with brutal encounters to see if any are worthy of becoming a consort or successor, seeking entertainment by driving wedges between allies. Meanwhile, the legendary vampire hunter Rudolph van Richten operates in the shadows, waiting for his moment to strike.

docs.title: Notable Reference Pages
docs.text:
• What Barovians Know: Page 26
  • Vistani Lore: Page 26
  • Random Encounters: Page 29

docs.title: The Tarokka Card Readings
docs.text:
During a fateful encounter with Madam Eva, five cards were drawn to illuminate the path toward defeating the darkness:

  • Card 1 (The Hooded One / 7 of Swords): This card speaks of history, revealing a faceless god waiting at the end of a long, winding mountain road. It indicates that the Tome of Strahd is hidden deep within the Amber Temple.
  • Card 2 (The Abjurer / 4 of Stars): Symbolizing great hope, protection, and a powerful force for good, this card points toward a fallen house guarded by a great stone dragon. The Holy Symbol of Ravenkind can be found at the highest peak of Argynvostholt.
  • Card 3 (The Druid / 5 of Glyphs): A card of raw power and strength, it foretells of a weapon of vengeance: a sword of sunlight. The Sunsword lies at the base of the Gulthias tree atop a hill of graves on Yester Hill, where the ancient dead sleep and the ravens watch.
  • Card 4 (The Ghost): Shedding light on a powerful ally in the battle against the darkness, this card reveals a fallen paladin lingering like a phantom in a dead dragon's lair. Sir Klutz, a phantom warrior residing in Castle Ravenloft, will stand with the party.
  • Card 5 (The Marionette): Leading directly to the enemy, this card urges the adventurers to look to great heights. Strahd von Zarovich waits near the beating heart of Castle Ravenloft, atop the peak of the north tower.

docs.title: Session 1: The Invitation and the Mists
docs.text:
Following your recent exploits, the party settled down for a period of rest in the city of Neverwinter. During this time, Gundren Rockseeker and his brother were incredibly active after being rescued from the clutches of the Black Spider. The brothers successfully reopened the Wave Echo Cave mine, and business flourished as they eagerly auctioned off magical items to the highest bidders along the Sword Coast. By regularly escorting these high-value magical transports, you crossed paths with many prominent figures. Among them was Duchess Morwen of Daggerford, an avid collector of the arcane who frequently purchased the brothers' wares. Over time, you developed a close friendship with her, which ultimately resulted in an exclusive invitation to her estate.

While attending Duchess Morwen’s grand banquet, the hostess requested a favor: she asked the party to rid the town of a band of mysterious travelers who had set up camp just outside the castle walls. When you went to confront them, the travelers shared captivating stories of a noble prince and a terrible curse they desperately wished to lift. Moved by their plight, you agreed to accompany them to meet Madam Eva, who they claimed could explain everything.

The party set off toward the south inside the Vistani wagons. After a couple of days of uneventful travel, a thick, suffocating fog suddenly rolled in, swallowing the road. When the mists finally parted, you found yourselves enclosed within a dark, oppressive forest, eventually arriving at a set of towering iron gates that swung open and closed entirely on their own. That night, after establishing camp, Wilbin suffered through a series of terrifying experiences and a vivid fever dream, catching a glimpse of a dark figure looking down from a brooding castle at a party of newly arrived adventurers.

The next day, you pressed forward through the gloomy town of Barovia without stopping, passing by a set of deeply unsettling gallows before finally arriving at the Vistani camp nestled next to Tser Pool. After spending the evening singing and dancing with the welcoming Vistani, Madam Eva approached the group and offered to read your fortunes. You accepted, and she revealed five mystical Tarokka cards that laid out the perilous path ahead.

docs.title: Session 2: Whispers in the Village of Barovia
docs.text:
The party began by heading west to investigate Tser Falls, though the search yielded very little of note. As you continued along the road, you were ambushed by five ferocious Dire Wolves, but the party coordinated effectively and slew them all. Shortly after the combat, you spotted Ismark the Lesser hiding anxiously in the roadside bushes. Upon introducing himself, he begged the party to accompany him back to the village of Barovia to help protect his sister, Ireena, and escort her away from the watchful gaze of Strahd.

Upon entering the dreary village of Barovia, you paid a visit to the local inn. The tavern keeper was remarkably grumpy and made absolutely no effort to speak with you, while three Vistani patrons sitting nearby showed a similar lack of interest in conversation. Leaving the tavern, you made your way to the Burgomaster’s mansion, only to find a somber scene: the Burgomaster was already dead, his body resting in a coffin on the floor. There you met Ireena Kolyana, who revealed that Strahd had already visited her twice and bitten her, though her memories of the encounters remained incredibly hazy.

Seeking more information about the town, you visited Mad Mary’s house. The weeping woman explained that her daughter, Gertruda, had run away into the night. She gave the party a painting of Gertruda along with a strange doll bearing the eerie inscription, "Is no fun, is no Blinsky!" With the doll and painting in hand, you returned to the Burgomaster's mansion to get a good night's rest. The party resolved to help transport the late Burgomaster’s coffin to the local church the following morning, and Ireena agreed to honor her brother’s wishes to flee the village as soon as her father was properly laid to rest.

docs.title: Session 3: The Church and the Pastry Cart
docs.text:
The morning began with a somber procession to the village church, where you met the local priest, Father Donavich. The party informed the distressed priest of their radical plan: they would attempt to put down his vampiric son, Doru, and then immediately revive him. Donavich reluctantly agreed to the attempt and headed out to the cemetery alongside Ireena and Ismark to begin burying the Burgomaster.

The party descended into the dark undercroft, successfully executing the plan by killing and subsequently reviving Doru. Overjoyed by his son's salvation, Father Donavich wept as Doru provided vital intelligence regarding Strahd and a mysterious figure known as the "Mad Mage." Before you departed, Donavich pulled the party aside to reveal a deep secret: Ireena was not the biological daughter of the Burgomaster, but an orphan he had found abandoned at the edge of the woods and adopted. This came as a shock to the party, as neither Ismark nor Ireena had any knowledge of her true heritage.

As you stepped outside the church, Wilbin noticed an old woman peddling homemade pies from a small pushcart. Driven by curiosity, Wilbin immediately bought and wolfed one down, which caused him to instantly plunge into vivid hallucinations. Seizing a chaotic opportunity, Ulvor secretly snatched several more pastries from the cart just before Kaz set the entire wooden cart ablaze, sending the old woman running away in terror.

The party gathered themselves and set off west down the road. In an attempt to lift spirits, a few jokes were shared before Gillian decided to play the bagpipes. To everyone's utter dismay, the performance was so jarring that it completely incapacitated the still-recovering Wilbin. Needing a change of pace, the group decided to veer off the main path to investigate a lonely windmill standing in the distance.

docs.title: Session 4: Old Bonegrinder
docs.text:
The party approached and cautiously entered the ancient windmill. Inside, Wilbin encountered Morgantha and quickly purchased some more of her addictive pies. However, when questioned about the specific ingredients used in her baking, the old woman stubbornly refused to answer. Suspecting foul play, Ulvor tried to aggressively push past her daughters to investigate the upper floors of the structure.

A fierce, confined battle immediately erupted. The hags unleashed their dark magic, but the party pushed through the assault, ultimately killing Morgantha and her two wicked daughters. Ascending the stairs to search the upper levels, you discovered a pair of terrified children locked inside small cages. The rescued children instantly recognized Ireena and Ismark, bringing immense relief to the siblings. Before abandoning the foul windmill, you searched the cupboards and uncovered three mysterious vials filled with potent poisons and magical mixtures. Securing the items and keeping the children close, the expanded group set off down the road toward the walled town of Vallaki.

docs.title: Session 5: The Shadows of Vallaki
docs.text:
Through your shared hardships and triumphs across the valley, the party successfully grew in power and reached level 6!

Upon arriving at the gates of Vallaki, you managed to convince the suspicious guards to grant you entry. Your first stop was the Blue Water Inn, where you left Ismark, Ireena, and the two rescued children to secure rooms and unpack while the rest of the party set out to explore the settlement. Heading into the local stockyard, you conducted a quick sweep of the area and noticed a peculiar carnival wagon belonging to an enigmatic showman named Rictavio. From there, you walked to the bustling town square, where you witnessed the imposing Izek Strazni putting up new festival signs. You took some time to interview the miserable citizens trapped in the stocks and those gathering water from the central fountain, learning about the iron-fisted rule of the Baron and the brewing political opposition led by Lady Wachter.

Realizing the town held deep secrets, you made your way to the local church and met with Father Lucian. The panicked priest confided in you that the sacred bones of St. Andral, which protected the church from vampire intrusion, had been stolen. You cross-examined the church hand, Milivoj, who eventually confessed that he had stolen the relics in exchange for coin provided by Henrik van der Voort, the local coffin maker.

The party marched directly to the coffin maker's shop and broke down the front door. Cornered, Henrik confessed that he had dangerous "visitors" staying upstairs in the storage room where the bones were hidden. Moving cautiously into the upper loft, Ulvor pried open a large wooden crate, immediately triggering an ambush as the bloodthirsty vampire spawn hiding within lunged out at the party.

docs.title: Session 6: Fire and Bones
docs.text:
The upper floor of the coffin maker's shop erupted into chaos as the vampire spawn attacked. A brutal, destructive battle followed, during which the party unleashed a devastating barrage of fire and lightning magic. While the onslaught successfully turned the tide and eradicated the vampire spawn, the sheer arcane force destroyed half of the wooden building in a roaring inferno.

Amid the smoking rubble of the upstairs cupboards, you successfully recovered the precious bones of St. Andral along with a small pouch of hidden coins. The party rushed back to the church and returned the relics to a profoundly grateful Father Lucian, ensuring the spiritual wards over the sanctuary were restored. With the immediate crisis averted, you headed back to the safety of the Blue Water Inn to reconvene, rest, and discuss your next moves with Ireena, Ismark, and the children.

docs.title: Session 7: The Road to Berez and Argynvostholt
docs.text:
The party reunited with Ireena, Ismark, and the kids at the Blue Water Inn, finding that they had successfully organized comfortable lodgings for everyone upstairs. During your stay, you were introduced to the Martikov family: Urwin, Danika, and their young boys, Brom and Bay. After spending a restful night at the inn, you departed the following morning, making a stop at the church to leave Ireena and Ismark under the protective supervision of Father Lucian.

Free to travel unburdened, the party headed down the highway toward Krezk. Along the way, you encountered a ghostly skeleton knight riding a phantom warhorse; intrigued, you followed the apparition until it crumbled into dust, leaving a trail that pointed down the road toward the ruins of Berez. Following the lead, you arrived in the abandoned settlement of Berez, crossed the rushing river, and met a mysterious scout named Muriel. She warned you of the horrific witch Baba Lysaga, who resided deep within the Berez swamp inside a bizarre, creeping hut. Muriel also shared her firm belief that Baba Lysaga was operating in league with Strahd. Weighing the danger, you decided to postpone a confrontation, turn back toward Krezk, and stick to your original travel plan.

On the return journey, you opted to make a detour to inspect the grand Argynvostholt mansion. Arriving at the ruined estate, you were greeted by a massive, weathered statue of a silver dragon sitting prominently outside. However, the moment you stepped forward to cross the threshold and enter the mansion, the stone dragon's mouth suddenly gaped open, unleashing a long, freezing gust of ice-cold air across the party.

docs.title: Session 8: The Haunted Manor
docs.text:
Bracing against the supernatural chill, the party pushed past the dragon statue and entered the mansion, moving through the grand main foyer and into the dark dining room. Inside, your attention was drawn to an ornate chandelier that cast a bright, warm illumination despite having absolutely no candles lit; Wilbin investigated the anomaly and determined that a permanent magical spell had been woven into the fixture.

Leaving the dining room behind, you cautiously entered the manor's desecrated chapel. There, you were ambushed by three vengeful undead knights, but through swift steel and divine power, you successfully laid the spirits to rest. With the immediate threats neutralized, you doubled back to systematically map the remainder of the ground floor. Your exploration led you straight into a dark chamber housing a massive horde of giant spiders, though the party coordinated seamlessly and easily dispatched the arachnids.

docs.title: Session 9: The Holy Symbol and the Dragon's Wrath
docs.text:
The morning began by clearing out the few remaining giant spiders nesting in the corners of the room. As you continued your search of the manor, you encountered the restless spirit of a dragon swirling within a large fireplace. Pressing deeper into the estate's bizarre interior, you discovered a gruesome sight: a severed head that bore an exact, terrifying resemblance to Gillian. Kevin immediately cast detect magic on the gruesome object, revealing that it was merely a cruel and sophisticated illusion. Shortly after, the party found a stray bat nesting inside an old ceramic pot that strangely seemed convinced Wilbin was its mother, followed by a tiny dragonet that burst from a hearth and flew rapidly up into the higher levels of the manor.

Ascending the stairs, you crossed paths with the spectral spirits of Sir Godfrey and Vladimir Horngaard. A grim and bitter phantom, Vladimir gruffly suggested that you speak with the Lord Commander, eventually giving a begrudging promise that he would aid the party against Strahd when the final battle arrived. Continuing to the absolute apex of the manor's tower, you hit a major milestone: you discovered the Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.

The party paused to take a short, well-deserved rest, during which Kevin sat quietly and successfully attuned to the powerful holy relic. Eager to test its capabilities and share the triumph, Kevin suggested they show the artifact to Vladimir Horngaard. However, Vladimir was completely unamused when Kevin accidentally shone a blinding ray of pure sunlight straight into his spectral face. Perceiving the act as a hostile insult, the ancient knight drew his greatsword and furiously attacked the group.

docs.title: Session 10: The Lightning Tower
docs.text:
The party managed to de-escalate the situation and narrowly avoid an all-out battle with the furious Vladimir Horngaard. Seizing the opportunity, you quickly evacuated the castle and hurried back onto the trail toward Krezk. As night fell, you established a secure campsite, sheltering comfortably under the magical protection of Gillian’s conjured hut while enjoying a feast of dream pies. During the quiet evening hours, Gillian experimented with his bagpipes and made a spectacular discovery: the instrument could be utilized effectively as both a sonic weapon and a musical outlet.

The next day, the party arrived at a major crossroads. Taking pride in public works, Wilbin took some time to repair a broken signpost, ensuring that future travelers could find their way through Barovia. From the intersection, you directed your steps north toward a lonely tower standing by the shores of Lake Baratok. Upon arrival, you noticed a colorful Vistani wagon parked outside. Kaz stepped forward and attempted to open the tower's heavy front door by turning the handle, inadvertently triggering a massive magical trap. Jagged arcs of lightning flashed violently up and down the length of the masonry, severely injuring Kaz, Gillian, and Kevin.

Remembering the cryptic clues you had gathered, Ulvor stepped up to the door and performed a precise, jiggly dance according to the ancient instructions, successfully unlocking the threshold. Inside the tower, the party solved a mechanical puzzle, figuring out how to command a set of stone statues to operate a central elevator platform. Riding it to the top floor, you discovered a furnished living space indicating that someone had recently lived here and meticulously tended to the grounds. You also uncovered a gruesome trophy hidden inside a chest: a severed Vistani head, which Ulvor casually claimed and packed away. However, your triumph was cut short when you peered out the high windows to find a massive, snarling pack of wolves and werewolves waiting for you on the ground below.

docs.title: Session 11: Werewolves and Mountain Passes
docs.text:
Standing at the base of the tower, the fierce werewolf alpha Kiril stepped forward and loudly demanded that the party throw down their weapons and submit to a mandatory visit with Count Strahd. Completely ignoring the threat, the party launched a coordinated assault from the tower, initiating a bloody skirmish that resulted in the slaughter of nearly the entire pack. A single werewolf named Nala survived the onslaught; cornered, she shared vital intelligence regarding their pack dynamics, explaining that Kiril's leadership was currently being challenged by a rival named Emil. She also revealed the precise location of their hidden den located far to the west of the tower.

Following a brief rest to bandage your wounds, Ulvor approached the Vistani wagon parked outside and attempted to pry it open. The moment he pulled down the main external lever, the entire vehicle exploded in a brilliant flash of fire, knocking both Wilbin and Gillian completely unconscious. The party scrambled to administer healing magic and, once stabilized, began traveling south toward a landmark that Bloodwing had previously spotted from the air.

As you traveled along the mountain path, you were approached by a cloaked, remarkably camp servant of Strahd who politely delivered a formal, written invitation to dine safely at Castle Ravenloft. Pressing past the encounter, the road eventually led you to the treacherous Tsolenka Pass. Using your magical resources, you successfully dispelled a roaring wall of green flame blocking the path, allowing you to enter an ancient guard tower where you discovered nothing but old, dust-covered skeletons.

docs.title: Session 12: Infiltration of the Amber Temple
docs.text:
The party began by taking a long, uninterrupted rest inside the relative safety of the Tsolenka Pass guard tower. Resuming your journey, you crossed a high bridge spanning a deep river canyon, passing by a motionless, mounted figure who watched your progress in eerie silence. You followed the steep, winding mountain road until you reached a fork in the trail; steering north, you marched until a massive, sinister temple carved with six colossal amber statues loomed out of the snow.

The moment you stepped through the grand entrance of the Amber Temple, a deadly ambush was sprung. A trio of floating, cackling flameskulls and a bizarre, foxlike creature that had taken refuge inside the hollow head of a giant statue opened fire on the group. A frantic battle ensued; while you successfully destroyed the flameskulls, the cunning foxlike entity managed to unleash a wave of magic that completely paralyzed Kevin, Gillian, and Kaz before fleeing deeper into the complex.

Once the paralysis wore off, the party located a hidden secret passage. Exploring the narrow corridor, you discovered the frozen corpse of a long-dead wizard. The corpse was still tightly clutching a wand of secrets, which Alex carefully retrieved for the party's use. Eager to explore the upper reaches of the chamber, Wilbin stepped forward and began formulating a plan to scale one of the massive amber statues so he could peer through its hollow eye holes.

docs.title: Session 13: The Vaults of Cold and Fire
docs.text:
Wilbin’s initial attempts to scale the smooth amber statue ended in disaster as he lost his footing and fell hard to the floor multiple times. Growing frustrated, he cast spider climb, allowing him to easily stroll up the masonry to inspect the eye holes before returning safely to the ground. The ambient temperature within the temple continued to plummet, forcing the party to make saving throws against extreme, supernatural cold. Wilbin succumbed to the chill, dropping to level 2 exhaustion, while Ulvor managed to fare slightly better, remaining at level 1 exhaustion.

As you crept through the quiet corridors, you spotted a group of wicked witches snooping around the doors and experimenting with various passwords. The party sprang into action, swiftly executing the witches and smashing their animated flying brooms to splinters. Afterward, you used your magical resources to dispel an arcane lock spell sealing one of the heavy doors.

Behind the threshold lay a grim chamber filled with ancient stone sarcophagi—and another trio of deadly flameskulls. A furious firefight erupted, and the hags' fiery magic nearly brought the party to the brink of death before you finally extinguished them. The toll of the temple was severely draining: Wilbin and Ulvor were now suffering from level 2 exhaustion, while Kevin and Gillian were weighed down by level 1 exhaustion. Seeking a silver lining, you explored an upper room and discovered a dormant Shield Guardian standing ready for a master. The grueling expedition paused as you halted your exploration at chamber X40.

docs.title: Session 14: Dark Gifts and Darker Desires
docs.text:
The party resumed their exploration and discovered a grand chamber overflowing with glittering treasure, though a closer inspection revealed a menacing, invisible statue standing guard over the hoard; wisely, you opted to leave the wealth completely untouched. Moving into an adjacent room, you were suddenly ambushed by four malevolent specters that telekinetically hurled broken junk and debris from the floorboards to batter the group. You laid the spirits to rest and thoroughly searched the room, uncovering a valuable scroll of wall of fire and a powerful staff of frost. Upon claiming the staff, Wilbin's mind was warped by its dark magic, causing him to gain a terrible new flaw: an insatiable, overwhelming crave for power above all else.

Deep within the temple's hidden recesses, you also discovered an incredibly detailed, miniature architectural model of Castle Ravenloft. Descending to the lower levels, the party crossed paths with a group of strange, babbling nothics and managed to engage them in conversation, extracting information about the layout of the temple. Following their guidance, you made your way toward the legendary Amber Vault. However, before you could claim its secrets, you had to fight for your lives against six bloodthirsty vampire spawn that were nesting in the dark, successfully defeating them after a fierce struggle.

docs.title: Session 15: The Tome of Strahd
docs.text:
Inside the eerie silence of the Amber Vault, Wilbin approached and boldly touched one of the glowing amber sarcophagi. The dark vestige trapped within spoke directly into his mind, whispering that he must return only when he had grown far more powerful. Realizing the party was utterly spent, Gillian cast his magical hut right there within the vault, allowing everyone to enjoy a safe, undisturbed long rest.

The next morning, you ascended to the temple's ancient library. After a meticulous search of the dusty shelves, you skyrocketed your resources by uncovering your primary objective: the legendary Tome of Strahd, alongside several other rare books and arcane spellbooks. Thoroughly exploring the remaining wings of the complex, you navigated a series of hidden secret passages, discovering a strange V-shaped amulet hidden directly behind a blackboard in a grand auditorium. Your exploration was violently interrupted when a massive flesh golem blindsided the group, brutally thwomping Wilbin once again before you managed to tear it apart. Afterward, you cleared out a pack of foul ghasts lurking at the termination of a secret tunnel, before finally returning to the massive piles of treasure you had bypassed earlier to conduct some serious, well-deserved looting.

docs.title: Session 16: Treasures of the Temple
docs.text:
With the temple largely cleared of immediate threats, the party focused on organizing their massive haul of treasure. You took time to fully activate and command your new Shield Guardian, whom you officially named Shaloth. Shortly after, you crossed paths with your old foxlike adversary; the party left no room for escape this time, trapping and swiftly killing the creature.

An inspection of the fox's remains yielded a pair of magical spectacles and a highly versatile robe of useful items, both of which were claimed by the party. To solve the logistical problem of transporting your massive haul of coins and relics, you packed the most valuable treasures into a sturdy chest and commanded the tireless Shaloth to carry it. With your powerful new Shield Guardian marching in lockstep, the party prepared to finally exit the freezing depths of the mountain temple.

docs.title: Session 17: The Roc of Tsolenka Pass
docs.text:
The heavily laden party began their descent from the peaks, heading back down the snow-covered mountain road toward Tsolenka Pass. However, as you were cautiously making your way across the high, narrow bridge, a shadow fell over the chasm as a colossal, monstrous bird materialized from the clouds and swooped down to attack. The massive beast dove directly into the party's ranks, snapping its beak and talons before seizing Kevin in its massive claws and lifting him into the air.

Refusing to watch his ally be carried off, Ulvor made a daring leap, jumping clean off the bridge to land squarely onto the monstrous bird's back. A dramatic battle in mid-air ensued as the party unleashed ranged attacks from the bridge. Together, you managed to deal a fatal blow to the creature, but as it died, it dropped Kevin into the roaring river far below. Reacting instantly, Wilbin cast feather fall to slow Kevin's rapid descent, while Ulvor fearlessly jumped down into the canyon after him. After scrambling down the treacherous cliffs to the shoreline, the rest of the party reunited with Kevin and Ulvor by the water's edge, where you established a secure camp and took a much-needed long rest by the lakeside.

docs.title: Session 18: The Doppelgänger and the Winery
docs.text:
Following a restorative long rest by the lake, the party packed up camp and resumed their march toward the village of Krezk. As you traveled along the road, you stumbled upon a gruesome sight: a mangled corpse that looked exactly like Ulvor. Shaken but solemn, Ulvor paid his respects by leaving a handaxe and a small arrangement of flowers on his own dead double, while Gillian provided a beautiful, haunting melody on his instrument to mark the eerie occasion.

After a full morning of travel, you finally arrived at the gates of Krezk, but the suspicious guards refused to grant you entry, calling for the Burgomaster instead. The Burgomaster appeared and offered a strict bargain: he would consider opening the gates to Krezk only after the party secured a shipment of wine from the nearby Wizard of Wines winery, which had gone mysteriously dark.

The party immediately diverted to the Wizard of Wines vineyard, where you encountered a stressed Davian Martikov. He explained that a hostile force of evil druids and corrupted blights had seized the winery and begged for your assistance. The conversation was cut short as a massive horde of twig and needle blights, led by a dark druid, swarmed out of the vineyards to attack. The party fought back fiercely, obliterating the plant monsters and successfully capturing the druid alive for questioning.

docs.title: Session 19: Clearing the Vineyards
docs.text:
The party immediately began interrogating the captured druid, demanding answers about their presence at the vineyard. She revealed that they had launched this assault as a counter-attack after being harassed by a mysterious league of "ravens." She further explained that her circle had stolen a priceless "treasure" from these ravens, who had subsequently been trying to steal it back. You bound and gagged the prisoner for later use, but she was unfortunately killed during a chaotic skirmish shortly thereafter.

The party then systematically cleared the entire winery building of remaining druids and blights. During your search of the facility, you discovered a functional printing press and a whimsical toy horse crafted by the legendary toymaker Blinsky. You also uncovered a detailed shipping manifest outlining exactly where the winery's shipments were traditionally delivered. Deep within the building, you encountered a particularly deranged druid wielding a twisted Gulthias staff; you subdued him, stripped him of the powerful artifact, and securely tied him up. Moving down into the dark wine cellar, you fought off another druid and a fresh wave of blights, discovering a hidden secret cold-room filled with brown mold that was being cleverly utilized as a natural refrigerator. With the facility secure, you headed back outside to speak with Davian, intending to ask him about the stolen treasure and warn him about a vat of wine you discovered had been poisoned.

docs.title: Session 20: The Ritual of Yester Hill
docs.text:
Davian Martikov expressed immense gratitude to the party for liberating the winery, and he acknowledged the grim news that one of their primary vats of wine had indeed been severely poisoned. He shared the deep history of the winery, explaining how the estate had been passed down through the generations of the Martikov family. Davian then revealed the secret to their success: three magical gems that uniquely allowed the vineyards to grow in the harsh Barovian climate. Tragically, all three had been stolen. One gem remained completely unaccounted for, though Urwin back in Vallaki reportedly held more information. The second had likely been seized by the swamp witch Baba Lysaga, and the third had been taken by the dark druids of Yester Hill.

The party immediately marched southwest toward Yester Hill, sending Bloodwing soaring ahead to scout the terrain. Halfway to the destination, you paused to take a long rest to prepare for the coming conflict. Upon resuming the approach, Gillian and Wilbin utilized the dense brush to travel stealthily through the woods, while the rest of the party marched openly down the main road.

At the foot of the hill, you were shocked to encounter Strahd von Zarovich himself, who casually reiterated his formal invitation to dinner at his castle before vanishing. Pushing up the steep incline, Gillian used his magic to disguise himself, blending in and dancing among the frenzied druids to disrupt their ritual. Suddenly, a colossal, terrifying Tree Blight burst forth from a massive wooden statue on the hilltop. Kaz reacted instantly, shattering the captured Gulthias staff over his knee, which unleashed a magical feedback loop that successfully killed the massive plant monstrosity. However, the battlefield remained highly volatile as a large force of wild Berserkers and corrupted Druids swarmed in to surround the party.

docs.title: Session 21: The Sunsword Awakens
docs.text:
The battle atop Yester Hill raged on, but through sheer martial prowess and arcane might, the party successfully defeated all the remaining druids and berserkers. Searching the battlefield, Wilbin sifted through the smoking remains of the massive tree blight and uncovered a beautifully glowing green crystal—the stolen magical gem from the winery. To the southwest of the hill, you discovered a twisted tree with a magical battleaxe embedded deep within its trunk; you successfully pried the weapon loose, identifying it as Treebane. Following a hunch, you began digging beneath the nearby skeleton of a long-dead adventurer, uncovering a large, ornately decorated box. Inside lay your grandest discovery yet: the legendary Sunsword.

This monumental triumph pushed the party's capabilities to new heights, and you officially reached level 8!

Kaz immediately sat down to commune with the sentient weapon, successfully attuning to the Sunsword. Standing on the western edge of the hill, you looked out over the roiling fog banks and caught a glimpse of a strange, distant city shimmering through the mists. Turning back, you returned to the winery and presented the glowing green crystal to Davian. He shared some lore regarding the crystals and the nature of the valley's choking mists, confirming that Baba Lysaga was indeed the keeper of the next gem. With your mission accomplished and a fresh wagon of unpoisoned wine secured, the party set off once more toward the gates of Krezk.

docs.title: Session 22: The Gates of Krezk
docs.text:
The party journeyed back toward Krezk with the wine wagon in tow. As you approached the village bridge, you stumbled upon a horrific scene: a body that had been brutally ripped to pieces, which turned out to be the tragic remains of a young child. Showing deep respect, you placed the small child's remains into the stone sarcophagus you had transported all the way from the Amber Temple, burying them properly to the somber, echoing tunes of Gillian's bagpipes.

You pressed onward to the gates of Krezk, hailing the guards and speaking once more with Burgomaster Krezkov. True to his word, he opened the gates and welcomed you inside, explaining that Krezk was a strictly self-sufficient village harboring no inns or taverns. He shared details about the imposing Abbey of Saint Markovia overlooking the town, the mysterious Abbot who ruled over it, and the legacy of Saint Markovia herself.

While exploring the north side of the village, you discovered a serene pool and a holy shrine; Kaz stepped forward and drank deeply from the blessed waters. The Burgomaster then introduced the party to a local named Balthazar, who provided your very first descriptive account of the bizarre "mongrelfolk" inhabiting the region. Suspecting foul play in the local cemetery, the party organized a midnight stakeout at the Burgomaster's house to catch a ring of suspected graverobbers. After midnight, your patience paid off as two shadowed figures crept toward the estate carrying shovels. The party ambushed and successfully captured them, revealing them to be two strange mongrelfolk. You secured the prisoners, slept through the remainder of the night to gain a long rest, and prepared to interrogate them in the morning.

docs.title: Session 23: The Abbot's Bride
docs.text:
Date: October 23, 2024

The morning began with the interrogation of your two unusual prisoners, who identified themselves as Zygfrek and Otto Belview. They shared a tragic tale, explaining how the entire Belview family had been abducted from the village ten years prior by the mysterious Abbot. To verify their story, you escorted them to their old, abandoned family plot; the Burgomaster positively identified the siblings and confirmed ownership of the house.

Resolved to investigate the mountaintop monastery, the party headed up the steep path to the Abbey of Saint Markovia. Upon arrival, you sent Bloodwing to snoop around the perimeter to scout for danger. Pushing into the courtyard, you discovered dozens of deformed mongrelfolk locked away inside rows of wooden sheds.

Stepping into the main hall, you finally met the enigmatic Abbot alongside a quiet woman named Vasilka. Gillian immediately stepped forward and shared a bizarre, courtly dance with Vasilka. The Abbot openly explained his horrific project: he was meticulously sewing together a flesh-golem bride to present to Strahd in an attempt to satisfy the vampire's curse, pointing out her striking, uncanny resemblance to Tatyana. Realizing the absolute madness and horror of the situation, weapons were drawn and initiative was rolled!

docs.title: Session 24: Clash at the Abbey
docs.text:
Date: December 18, 2024

The confrontation at the Abbey began with a terrifying transformation as the Abbot shed his mortal guise, revealing himself to be a fallen angel adorned with massive, radiant wings. Simultaneously, the flesh-golem bride Vasilka let out a horrific, blood-curdling screech and lunged forward to attack the party.

The battle escalated instantly into a clash of titans when Gillian cast polymorph to transform himself into a colossal giant ape—only for the celestial Abbot to counter by morphing into a massive giant ape himself. A brutal wrestling match echoed through the stone hall as the party focused their fire on the angelic horror. Through coordinated strikes and immense physical force, you ultimately defeated the Abbot. The moment the celestial fell, the magic animating Vasilka collapsed, causing the golem to dramatically fall apart into a gruesome pile of dislodged body parts. With the primary threats neutralized, you cautiously ascended the grand staircase of the main hall, where you encountered a lone mongrelfolk casually playing music, sitting adjacent to a large, mysterious object concealed beneath a dusty cloth sheet.

docs.title: Session 25: Madness in the Courtyard
docs.text:
Date: January 15, 2025

The party approached the musical mongrelfolk, who identified himself as Clovin Belview. Through careful diplomacy and a bit of trickery, you convinced him to head down into the Abbey's cellar to fetch more wine for the group. However, the moment Clovin descended into the darkness, you heard him screaming in pure terror as he bolted back out into the open courtyard. In the confusion, the Abbey bells were accidentally rung; the moment the iron knell echoed over the peaks, the entire population of locked-up mongrelfolk began screaming in a horrific, unified chorus of "Food, food!!"

The Abbey erupted into absolute chaos. The party moved swiftly through the rooms to clear out emerging threats. Wilbin playfully jumped onto a row of old beds, inadvertently disturbing a nest of malevolent shadows that swarmed out to attack, though you quickly destroyed them. While searching the upper chambers, you discovered a woman named Ezmerelda d'Avenir hidden in one of the rooms. She revealed that she was a renowned vampire hunter who had infiltrated the abbey while searching for her missing mentor, Rudolph van Richten. Recognizing a common cause, she agreed to rendezvous with the party back at the lightning tower at a later date.

Regrouping, you moved out into the central courtyard and killed a massive wave of frenzied mongrelfolk that had broken loose from the sheds. As you attempted to secure the eastern hallway, your progress was blocked by yet another massive flesh golem, backed by a fresh swarm of aggressive mongrelfolk.

docs.title: Session 26: Return to Vallaki
docs.text:
Date: February 12, 2025

The party systematically swept through the remaining rooms of the Abbey, checking on the remaining pockets of Mongrelfolk. During the sweep, Ulvor discovered a small group of them hoarding a golden statuette; through quick bartering, you managed to exchange a platinum ring to claim the relic. Kaz spoke to the raven in the garden, learning crucial information about the second magical winery gem—the bird confirmed that Baba Lysaga had used its vibrant magic to physically animate her creeping swamp hut. Meanwhile, Kevin took custody of the newly acquired golden statuette, discovering that it possessed a powerful blessing granting him a +1 bonus to all saving throws.

With the Abbey completely cleared and secured, you marched back down the mountain into the village and met with the Burgomaster. You explained the horrific reality of what had occurred within the monastery walls and officially set your two mongrelfolk prisoners, Zygfrek and Otto, free. Deeply relieved that the shadow over his town had been lifted, the Burgomaster declared that the party would always be safely welcomed in Krezk from that day forward.

Wishing to check on your allies, you traveled back down the highway to Vallaki. Your first stop was St. Andral’s Church, where you confirmed that Ireena and Ismark were still safe under the priest's care. From there, you headed to the Blue Water Inn and reconvened with Urwin Martikov. You explained how you had successfully liberated his family’s winery, but Urwin once again adamantly denied any personal involvement or knowledge regarding the missing gems. You conducted a thorough sweep of the area to locate the first missing stone but came up empty-handed. After enjoying a long rest and a peaceful night's sleep, the party resolved to visit the local Vistani camp to purchase vital supplies before finally honoring Strahd's lingering dinner invitation.

docs.title: Session 27: The Rescue of Arabelle
docs.text:
Date: March 11, 2025

The party traveled to the Vistani camp situated southwest of Vallaki, discovering a population of somber dusk elves living alongside the Vistani. You spoke at length with the dusk elves and gathered crucial lore about their tragic history. You learned of Patrina Velikov, sister to their current leader Kasimir Velikov, who had once been a promised bride to Strahd before she was murdered by her own people to prevent her damnation. In a fit of monstrous revenge, Strahd slaughtered every single woman and child among the dusk elves as punishment. Kasimir Velikov then spoke extensively about the Amber Temple, sharing his absolute certainty that the remote facility held the ultimate secret to Strahd's power, detailing how ancient wizards had locked away evil vestiges there long ago. He expressed a fierce desire to see the vampire lord utterly destroyed.

Turning your attention to the Vistani camp, you met a distraught leader named Luvash, who explained that his young daughter, Arabelle, had mysteriously vanished the previous day. Luvash handed you a doll that belonged to the girl to help aid your search. Utilizing a pack of trained mastiffs, you followed the scent of the doll all the way to the shores of Lake Baratok, north of Vallaki.

The party secured a couple of old rowing boats and paddled out onto the misty waters toward a lone figure fishing in the distance. As you drew near, Kaz spotted a little girl tightly bound in ropes resting at the bottom of the boat. The fisherman, a destitute local drunk named Bluto, suddenly panicked and threw the tied-up Arabelle into the deep water to drown as a sacrifice. Kaz reacted instantly, diving headfirst into the freezing lake and swimming downward to pull the sinking girl back to the surface. You successfully rescued Arabelle, who immediately and sassily demanded her doll back. The party returned the child safely to Luvash, who was profoundly grateful. In exchange for his daughter's life, Luvash offered the party full access to his trading wares, prompting you to draw up a shopping list for healing potions, diamonds for revivify, and other critical magical gear.

docs.title: Session 28: Carriage to Castle Ravenloft
docs.text:
Date: March 25, 2025

The day began with Wilbin thoroughly searching the body of the captured Bluto, uncovering nothing but a half-empty bottle of cheap wine. With the reward and favor from Luvash secured, the party finalized their extensive shopping and trading lists. Kaz arranged to sell a collection of weapons including a greataxe, a longbow, a morningstar, and a shield for 50 gold pieces, while Ulvor sold an ornate scepter and Kevin traded away a set of golden hawks. In return, the party organized their dream acquisition list: Kaz sought a clockwork amulet and a cloak of protection; Kevin requested a pearl of power and an amulet of the devout; Ulvor selected a ring of jumping, a cloak of protection, and an assortment of high-tier healing potions; Wilbin opted for a pearl of power; and Gillian finalized his bardic magic items via a private message to Luvash.

Before leaving Vallaki, you made a final stop at Blinsky’s Toys to purchase some curiosities. The eccentric toymaker spoke about Rictavio, noting that the showman was a frequent visitor who had gifted him his pet monkey, and confirmed that Rictavio's carnival wagon was still parked in town. Blinsky also shared a deeply unsettling detail: another mysterious customer had been forcing him to construct custom dolls, and the latest batch was designed to look exactly like Ireena Kolyana. Finally, he shared rumors of a legendary "clock man" hidden somewhere within the walls of Castle Ravenloft, asking the party to keep an eye out for it.

With their preparations complete, the party set off toward the brooding silhouette of Castle Ravenloft. Upon reaching the dark crossroads, you found a grand black coach waiting in the mist with its doors swung open. The party climbed inside—with Ulvor boldly choosing to sit outside on the driver's bench—and the carriage automatically whisked you up the cliffs directly to the castle gates. Waiting in the courtyard was Strahd’s sinister chamberlain, Rahadin, who silently led the group inside to the grand dining room. There, an ominous cloaked figure sat with his back to you, playing a dramatic melody on a massive pipe organ as Rahadin coldly requested that the party take their seats.

docs.title: Session 29: Dinner and Gargoyles
docs.text:
Date: April 29, 2025

The heavy wooden doors clicked shut and locked behind you as the party took their seats to begin dinner. The mysterious figure seated at the organ turned to address the group, cordially inviting them to enjoy their meal and engaging in brief, mocking banter. Suddenly, the figure dissolved into thin air with a mocking, cackling laugh, revealing that the host was merely an illusion. Realizing the trap had sprung, the party wasted no time and used brute force to break down the locked doors leading back out to the main hall.

As you navigated the grand corridors, Kevin spotted a set of fine plate armor standing against the wall and immediately began donning the heavy steel pieces. However, the castle defenses reacted instantly as a flock of vicious stone gargoyles detached themselves from the architecture and swooped down to attack.

The party fought their way through the hall and retreated out into the open courtyard to take a short rest and wait for logistical reinforcements. Lowering the heavy iron drawbridge, you allowed your faithful Shield Guardian, Shaloth, to march into the castle compound to deliver your primary weapons and equipment chest. Re-armed and ready for a dungeon crawl, you stepped back inside the castle proper, only to be instantly caught in a hidden floor trap that triggered a sudden architectural shift, instantly separating the party and teleporting Kaz and Ulvor away into the dark, unknown depths of area K47.

docs.title: Session 30: The Beating Heart
docs.text:
Date: June 18, 2025

Separated from their allies, Wilbin, Gillian, and Kevin carefully advanced up a grand spiral staircase in search of the missing fighters. Further up the flights, they successfully reunited with Kaz and Ulvor, who were found standing in a daze, staring blankly at a haunting, enchanted portrait of Strahd von Zarovich.
