As the group consolidated, Wilbin noticed a fine carpet on the floor and attempted to lift it; the textile instantly surged to life as an animated rug of smothering, wrapping itself tightly around Wilbin and swallowing him whole. Simultaneously, the magical portrait flared to life, temporarily seizing telepathic control over Ulvor's mind. The party tore the rug to pieces and snapped Ulvor out of his trance.

Pressing forward, you ascended to the absolute peak of the stone stairs, coming face-to-face with a grand and terrifying sight: a massive, crystalline red heart floating in the center of the North Tower, beating with an eerie, rhythmic pulse. Deciding to explore lower levels before tackling the artifact, you navigated back down the spiral stairway.

You pushed through a bizarre room filled with dozens of stray cats, followed by a dark laboratory lined with shelves of glowing glass vials. There, a coven of wicked castle witches ambushed the group. The party fought fiercely, killing the hags and looting seven homebrew magical potions from their workstations. Driven by reckless curiosity, Wilbin immediately uncorked and drank one of the unknown mixtures, instantly poisoning himself. Groaning and recovering from the toxins, the party made their way further down the stairs, stepping cautiously into the castle's grand foyer.

docs.title: Session 31: The Catacombs Entrance
docs.text:
Date: September 16, 2025

The party officially transitioned onto the Virtual Tabletop (VTT) grid to navigate the complex lower architecture of the castle. As you crept through the shadows, you crossed paths with Escher, a sophisticated vampire consort of Strahd. Rather than attacking, he casually engaged the group and led you down into a massive bone room dominated by a colossal dragon skull mounted to the wall.

Turning south to scout the perimeter, the party was suddenly ambushed by ten ancient skeletons lurking in the darkness; you formed a defensive line and swiftly crushed the undead bones to dust. Turning north, you discovered a hidden secret passage that descended into a grand chamber housing a large, magical brazier surrounded by colored stones. Adjacent to the brazier room, you found a viewing platform holding two lonely chairs overlooking a vast abyss. Pushing down a narrow, damp passageway, Kevin stepped squarely onto a hidden pressure plate, instantly triggering a trap that dropped a heavy iron gate and securely locked him inside a cramped solitary cell. The rest of the party scrambled to block the remaining mechanism, desperately searching for a way around the architecture to free their trapped ally.

docs.title: Session 32: Crypts and Vampire Spawn
docs.text:
Date: October 13, 2025

The session was spent executing a frantic rescue mission to extract Kevin from his solitary cell. The party initially entered the vast castle catacombs to find an alternate route, but your path was blocked by rows of sealed tombs, noting a single, solitary coffin resting in a prominent alcove. Realizing the crypts didn't connect directly, you navigated through a series of flooded tunnels, utilizing the water channels to finally bypass the iron bars and reach Kevin's cell.

However, the rescue quickly devolved into magical chaos as Kaz stepped on a teleportation rune and was instantly zapped away into a separate locked cell; after freeing him, he managed to step on the rune a second time, instantly teleporting himself right back into confinement. During the chaotic exploration of the damp chambers, you uncovered a valuable relic: the Crusader's Shortsword.

After gathering the party back together, you approached the solitary coffin you had spotted earlier and pried it open, finding nothing inside but a luxurious, empty purple velvet lining. Seeking a way out, you pulled a heavy wall lever, which dramatically lowered a massive iron portcullis elsewhere in the dungeon. The moment the mechanism clicked into place, an ambush was sprung as three bloodthirsty vampire spawn materialized from the shadows, forcing the party into a desperate melee combat.

docs.title: Session 33: The Luck Blade and Royal Tombs
docs.text:
Date: December 16, 2025

Following the destruction of the vampire spawn, the party began a systematic exploration of the ancient castle crypts, opening several sealed tombs. One crypt was found to contain the skeletal remains of a legendary gnomish mage named Gralmore Nimblenobs; Wilbin carefully searched the dust and recovered a valuable bundle of arcane spell scrolls. Seeking to activate another mechanism, Wilbin inserted his brass-ended staff into a strange wall receptacle, triggering an immediate magical feedback loop that heavily zapped him with electricity. Undeterred, you opened an adjacent royal vault and fought off Strahd's legendary fiendish nightmare steed, Beucephalus, successfully slaying the hellish beast.

Utilizing detect magic to screen the remaining tombs, the party uncovered an incredible trove of legendary relics. Inside one crypt, you discovered a functional magical portal rune. In another, you hit a massive milestone: you uncovered a legendary Luck Blade shortsword, which still held exactly one reality-altering wish charge. Continuing your sweep, you discovered Saint Markovia's Thighbone (a potent weapon against the undead) and a complete Deck of Illusions.

Pushing deep into the absolute heart of the catacombs, you located the grand, somber tombs of King Barov von Zarovich and Queen Ravenovia van Roeyen. Finally, the party breached the sealed tomb of Sergei von Zarovich, where you discovered a pristine, magnificent set of magical plate armor resting atop his stone effigy.

docs.title: Session 34: The Light of Argynvostholt
docs.text:
Date: January 2026

The party returned to the grand bone room to retrieve the massive artifact they had discovered earlier. The moment you stepped into the chamber, a horde of castle horrors ambushed the group, triggering a frantic skirmish. During the opening round of combat, Alex took a massive blow and was completely knocked unconscious. The party rallied around their fallen ally, unleashing a barrage of divine and physical strikes that successfully dispatched the monsters. Once the chamber was secure and Alex was healed back to consciousness, you stepped up to the wall and used your combined strength to pry the massive dragon skull off its mounts.

While completing a final sweep of the immediate area, you successfully identified two more powerful items: the Icon of Ravenloft and a magical mace. Strapping the heavy dragon skull to the tireless shoulders of your Shield Guardian, Shaloth, the party executed a daring escape from Castle Ravenloft.

You marched through the gloomy Barovian wilderness all the way back to the ruined manor of Argynvostholt. Entering the grand estate, you carried the massive skull up to the central tower and returned the remains of the silver dragon to their rightful resting place. Instantly, a brilliant, supernatural beacon of pure, silvery light flared to life atop the manor's tower, piercing through the eternal mists and casting a ray of profound hope across the entire valley.

docs.title: Session 35: Tiger in the Stockyard
docs.text:
Date: March 16, 2026

With the inspiring beacon of Argynvostholt now burning bright in the sky, the party marched back to the Vistani camp southwest of Vallaki to cash in their favors and collect the gear they had previously negotiated. The trade was highly successful: you officially took possession of a pearl of power, a cloak of protection, a set of magical pipes, and a substantial crate of healing potions.

From the camp, you traveled into the town of Vallaki and checked back into the Blue Water Inn. That evening, Gillian put on an absolute masterclass performance in the tavern room. The show caught the attention of the mysterious Rictavio, who watched from the corner, thoroughly impressed by the bard's skill.

After closing time, you quietly followed Rictavio out to the town stockyard as he approached his heavily reinforced carnival wagon. Creeping up to the wooden bars, you spoke directly with the massive sabre-tooth tiger locked inside using speak with animals. The beast shared a chilling detail: it had been meticulously trained to hunt and kill Vistani, whom it described as "the direct servants of the absolute evil that controls these dark lands." Leaving Ulvor behind to keep a watchful eye on the stockyard, the rest of the party headed back to their rooms at the inn, formulating a plan to confront Rictavio directly over breakfast before securing a long rest.

docs.title: Session 36: The Ruins of Berez
docs.text:
Date: April 29, 2026

The next morning, the party sat down with Rictavio over a hearty breakfast at the inn and confronted him about his secret wagon. Cornered by your intelligence, the showman dropped his disguise and proudly revealed his true identity: he was indeed the legendary vampire hunter, Rudolph van Richten. You spent the morning discussing Strahd’s tactical weaknesses, and Van Richten gave a solemn vow that he would stand ready to join the party when the final moment came to confront the vampire lord at Castle Ravenloft.

With a powerful ally secured, the party marched south to deal with the remaining threat in the valley: Baba Lysaga. You arrived at the gloomy, swampy ruins of Berez and began a thorough exploration of the abandoned settlement. You inspected a crumbling, dilapidated mansion, finding nothing but rows of empty wine casks rotting away in the damp cellar.

Turning your attention toward the center of the marsh, you approached a bizarre, giant hut surrounded by rusted cages filled with screaming, flapping marsh birds. Working quickly, you managed to pry the cages open to free the birds before stepping forward and boldly opening the front door to Baba Lysaga's creeping domain.

docs.title: Session 37: The Witch of the Swamp
docs.text:
Date: May 6, 2026

The infiltration quickly turned into a deadly trap; Baba Lysaga was not caught off guard, but had been hiding magically in the surrounding swamp, waiting for the characters to step inside her threshold. The moment the party entered the structure, the ancient witch surged out from the briars and clambered into a massive, floating giant's skull. Flying rapidly up into the storm clouds, she cackled loudly and unleashed the full fury of her high-level arcane repertoire upon the group.

The battlefield became an absolute nightmare of high-tier magic. Baba Lysaga rained down devastating spells, rocking the party with power word stun, a lethal green ray of finger of death, and a suffocating cloud of cloudkill that blanketed the swamp water. Simultaneously, the ground churned as she summoned massive swarms of biting insects and a battalion of terrifying, animated scarecrows that marched out from the reeds to surround the bogged-down party.
